112	/-! ## §1. Depth of a finite module
113	
114	The `I`-depth of a finite `R`-module `M` is the supremum in
115	`{0, 1, 2, …, ∞}` of the lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in `I`
116	(provided `IM ≠ M`; if `IM = M` we set `depth_I(M) = ∞`). Mathlib `b80f227`
117	exposes the regular-sequence predicate `RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular`
118	(`Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.RegularSequence`) but not the resulting
119	numeric depth function — that is the gap this declaration fills.
120	
121	Blueprint reference: `def:depth` (Stacks tag 00LF). -/
122	
123	/-- The **`I`-depth** of a finite `R`-module `M`: the supremum (in `ℕ∞`) of
124	lengths of `M`-regular sequences contained in the ideal `I`.
125	
126	When `IM = M` (the "trivial-quotient" case, e.g. `M = 0` or `I = R`) the
127	supremum is taken to be `⊤` by convention. When `(R, 𝔪)` is local one usually
128	calls `depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M` simply *the depth* of `M`.
129	
130	iter-176+: the body is the supremum
131	```
132	sSup { (n : ℕ∞) | ∃ rs : List R, rs.length = n ∧ (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ I) ∧
133	                  RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs }
134	```
135	folded with the `IM = M` clause. The signature is non-tautological: it
136	asserts a function `(Ideal R) → (M : Type v) → ℕ∞` matching the Stacks
137	00LF definition.
138	
139	**iter-179 Mathlib-gap check (Lane F Target 2 STRETCH)**: a pinned-commit
140	audit of `Mathlib.RingTheory.Regular.Depth` (the only file under
141	`Mathlib/RingTheory/*` containing the word "depth") confirms that Mathlib
142	`b80f227` ships only depth-zero lemmas (`IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff`)
143	and *not* the numeric depth function itself. The one-liner re-export route
144	is therefore not available; the body stays a typed `sorry` until an
145	iter-180+ body lane fills the supremum-with-`IM=M` clause directly. -/
146	noncomputable def depth {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (_I : Ideal R)
147	    (_M : Type v) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : ℕ∞ :=
148	  open Classical in
149	  if _I • (⊤ : Submodule R _M) = ⊤ then (⊤ : ℕ∞)
150	  else sSup { n : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = n ∧
151	    (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ _I) ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular _M rs }
152	
153	end Module
154	
155	end RingTheory

157	/-! ## §2. Projective dimension
158	
159	Mathlib `b80f227` exposes the categorical
160	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension : C → WithBot ℕ∞` on an abelian category
161	with enough projectives (file `Mathlib.CategoryTheory.Abelian.Projective.Dimension`).
162	For `R`-modules this specialises to `ModuleCat.of R M`. The blueprint pins the
163	`Module.projectiveDimension` name as the re-export that downstream consumers
164	can use directly on an `R`-module without first packaging it in `ModuleCat`.
165	
166	Blueprint reference: `def:projective_dimension`. -/
167	
168	namespace Module
169	
170	/-- The **projective dimension** of an `R`-module `M`, defined as the
171	categorical projective dimension of `ModuleCat.of R M`.
172	
173	This is a re-export of `CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension` specialised to
174	the abelian category `ModuleCat R`. The categorical definition is the
175	infimum (in `WithBot ℕ∞`) of `n : ℕ` such that all `Ext^i(M, -)` vanish for
176	`i > n`, equivalently the smallest length of a projective resolution of `M`.
177	
178	Mathlib has the categorical `projectiveDimension` and the module-specific
179	`ModuleCat.projectiveDimension_eq_of_linearEquiv`; the blueprint pins the
180	wrapper name so downstream consumers can write
181	`Module.projectiveDimension R M` rather than threading `ModuleCat.of`.
182	
183	The body is the one-line re-export
184	`CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)`; iter-178 closed
185	this declaration kernel-clean. -/
186	noncomputable def projectiveDimension (R : Type u) [Ring R]
187	    (_M : Type u) [AddCommGroup _M] [Module R _M] : WithBot ℕ∞ :=
188	  CategoryTheory.projectiveDimension (ModuleCat.of R _M)
189	
190	end Module

196	/-! ## §3. Depth via Ext characterisation
197	
198	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a
199	nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the depth of `M` equals the smallest index `i`
200	at which `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero (Stacks tag 00LP).
201	
202	This is the lemma A.4.a's downstream consumer ultimately reads off:
203	combined with the regular-sequence definition (`depth`), the Ext
204	characterisation provides both the *lower bound* (regular sequences exhibit
205	`Ext^i = 0` for `i < length(rs)`) and the *upper bound* (failure of any
206	extension lifts a non-zero element in `Ext^{depth}(κ, M)`).
207	
208	The signature pins the equivalence via the depth-bound `↔` `Ext`-vanishing-
209	below: `n ≤ depth(M) ↔ Ext^i(κ, M) = 0 for all i < n`. This is logically
210	equivalent to "depth(M) = smallest i with Ext^i ≠ 0" and is the form most
211	convenient for inductive proofs (Stacks 00LP proof: pick `x ∈ 𝔪`
212	non-zero-divisor, use long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` on `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0`).
213	
214	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_via_ext` (Stacks tag 00LP). -/
215	
216	/-! ### Helper C (iter-183 Lane G, axiom-clean): `Ann`-killing of Ext via R-linearity
217	
218	For any `R`-modules `N, M` and any `x : R` in the annihilator of `N`, the
219	R-action `x • e` on `e : Ext^i_R(N, M)` is zero.
220	
221	Proof sketch: `x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i)` (by R-linearity:
222	`mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp`). For `x ∈ Ann(N)` the morphism
223	`x • 𝟙_N : N ⟶ N` is the zero map, so `mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N) = mk₀ 0 = 0`
224	(`mk₀_zero`), and `0.comp e = 0` (`zero_comp`).
225	
226	iter-183 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. This is the precise statement of the
227	Stacks-00LP "`x ∈ 𝔪` annihilates `Ext^*(κ, -)`" trick, lifted to the more
228	general `x ∈ Ann(N)` form so it covers both `N = κ` and `N = R/(x_1,…,x_k)`. -/
229	private lemma ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator
230	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R]
231	    {N M : ModuleCat.{u} R} {i : ℕ} (e : Abelian.Ext.{u} N M i)
232	    {x : R} (hx : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (N : Type u)) :
233	    x • e = 0 := by
234	  -- Step 1: x • 𝟙_N = 0 in ModuleCat (the underlying linear map sends m ↦ x • m,
235	  -- which is 0 since x ∈ Ann(N)).
236	  have hkill : (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N)) = (0 : N ⟶ N) := by
237	    apply ModuleCat.hom_ext
238	    apply LinearMap.ext
239	    intro n
240	    change x • n = 0
241	    exact Module.mem_annihilator.mp hx n
242	  -- Step 2: x • e = (mk₀ (x • 𝟙_N)).comp e (zero_add i) by R-linearity.
243	  have hreflect :
244	      (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))).comp e (zero_add i)
245	        = x • e := by
246	    have hmk : (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (x • (𝟙 N : N ⟶ N))
247	                : Abelian.Ext.{u} N N 0)
248	        = x • CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ (𝟙 N) :=
249	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_smul (R := R) x (𝟙 N)
250	    rw [hmk, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_comp,
251	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_id_comp]
252	  -- Step 3: substitute hkill to collapse mk₀ … to mk₀ 0 = 0, then zero_comp.
253	  rw [← hreflect, hkill, CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_zero,
254	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
255	
256	/-- **Depth via Ext characterisation.** For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`
257	with residue field `κ = R/𝔪` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the
258	depth-bound `n ≤ depth(M)` is equivalent to the vanishing of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)`
259	for all `i < n`. Equivalently, `depth(M)` is the smallest `i` at which
260	`Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is nonzero.
261	
262	iter-176+: the body proceeds by induction on `n` via the long exact sequence
263	of `Ext^*(κ, -)` applied to `0 → M → M → M/xM → 0` for a non-zero-divisor
264	`x ∈ 𝔪`. The base case `n = 0` is `Hom(κ, M) ≠ 0 ↔ depth(M) = 0`, which is
265	the standard "the maximal ideal contains a zero-divisor on `M` iff
266	`𝔪 ∈ Ass(M)`" characterisation (Stacks 00LC).
267	
268	iter-183 Lane G structural progress:
269	* Generalised the inductive hypothesis over `M` (so `ih` is universally
270	  quantified in `M`, allowing recursive use on `M/xM`).
271	* Base case `n = 0` closed kernel-clean (both directions trivial).
272	* New axiom-clean helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` (lines
273	  229–254) discharges substantive piece (a) of the Stacks 00LP proof:
274	  *"`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^*(N, ·)`"*, via
275	  `mk₀_smul + smul_comp + mk₀_id_comp + mk₀_zero + zero_comp`.
276	* The inductive step's **backward direction** has the regular-element
277	  extraction stage closed structurally: from the `Ext^0(κ, M) = 0`
278	  hypothesis we derive `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)` (via `mk₀_eq_zero_iff +
279	  ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff`) and then invoke
280	  `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` + `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`
281	  to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`, with
282	  `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` providing the `Nontrivial
283	  (M/xM)` instance for the recursive IH application.
284	
285	Residual `sorry`s (2 named inline branches):
286	1. **Forward direction**  `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` —
287	   needs: extract regular sequence of length n+1 from supremum (handle `⊤`
288	   via Nakayama); cons-decompose via `isRegular_cons_iff`; LES of Ext on
289	   `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`; recurse via `ih` on M/xM.
290	2. **Backward direction**, final assembly — after the regular-element
291	   extraction (already done), needs: LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on
292	   M/xM at indices `j < n` (using the new helper to kill the
293	   multiplication-by-x maps); apply `ih (M := M/xM)` to obtain regular
294	   sequence `rs'`; cons `x :: rs'`; conclude via `le_sSup`. -/
295	theorem depth_eq_smallest_ext_index
296	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
297	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
298	    [Nontrivial M] (n : ℕ) :
299	    (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M ↔
300	      ∀ i : ℕ, i < n →
301	        ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
302	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
303	            (ModuleCat.of R M) i, e = 0 := by
304	  -- We generalize `M` so the inductive hypothesis is universally quantified
305	  -- over the module — this lets the induction step recursively apply the IH
306	  -- to the quotient `M / xM` (a *different* module of the same shape).
307	  induction n generalizing M with
308	  | zero =>
309	    -- LHS: `(0 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` is `bot_le`.
310	    -- RHS: `∀ i < 0, …` is vacuous since no `i` satisfies `i < 0`.
311	    exact ⟨fun _ i hi _ => absurd hi (Nat.not_lt_zero i), fun _ => bot_le⟩
312	  | succ n ih =>
313	    -- The Stacks 00LP inductive step. The blueprint sketch is:
314	    --
315	    -- (⇒) Assume `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M`. Then `Nontrivial M` rules out
316	    --     `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` (Nakayama), so `depth M` is the supremum and we can
317	    --     extract an `M`-regular sequence `rs = x :: rs'` of length `n+1` in
318	    --     `𝔪`. The cons-decomposition `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`
319	    --     gives `IsSMulRegular M x` and `IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs'`.
320	    --     For `i = 0`: `Hom(κ, M) ↪ Hom(κ, M)` via `[x]` is `[x]` on the
321	    --     domain `Hom(κ, M)`, but `x ∈ 𝔪 = Ann(κ)` kills this on the κ side,
322	    --     so `Hom(κ, M) = 0`. Pass to `Ext^0` via `addEquiv₀`.
323	    --     For `1 ≤ i ≤ n`: the SES `0 → M →[x] M → M/xM → 0` (built via
324	    --     `IsSMulRegular.smulShortComplex_shortExact`) plus the fact that
325	    --     `[x]_*` is zero on `Ext^i(κ, M)` (because `x ∈ Ann κ` ⇒
326	    --     `x • 𝟙_κ = 0`, hence by `precomp_smul = smul_precomp` the
327	    --     R-action on `Ext^i(κ, M)` is annihilated by `x`) lets the
328	    --     LES connecting map `Ext^{i-1}(κ, M/xM) ↠ Ext^i(κ, M)` be
329	    --     surjective.  By IH applied to `M/xM` (we get `n ≤ depth (M/xM)`,
330	    --     so `Ext^j(κ, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n`) we conclude
331	    --     `Ext^i(κ, M) = 0` for `1 ≤ i ≤ n`.
332	    --
333	    -- (⇐) Assume `∀ i < n+1, ∀ e ∈ Ext^i(κ, M), e = 0`.
334	    --     Specialise at `i = 0` and use `Ext.addEquiv₀` to extract
335	    --     `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)`.  Apply
336	    --     `IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff` (Mathlib) with
337	    --     `N := ResidueField R` and `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) =
338	    --     maximalIdeal R` to obtain `x ∈ 𝔪` with `IsSMulRegular M x`.
339	    --     The SES + same "x annihilates Ext^*(κ, ·)" fact give
340	    --     `Ext^j(κ, M/xM) = 0` for `j < n` (via the LES at indices `j ≤ n-1`).
341	    --     `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` is `Nontrivial` by
342	    --     `nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal` and `Module.Finite`
343	    --     as a quotient.  Apply `ih` on `M/xM` at index `n` to get a
344	    --     regular sequence `rs'` of length `n` in `𝔪` on `M/xM`.  Then
345	    --     `x :: rs'` is a regular sequence of length `n+1` in `𝔪` on `M`
346	    --     by `RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff`. This gives
347	    --     `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup` on the depth supremum.
348	    --
349	    -- iter-183: the helper `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` above closes
350	    -- the substantive piece (a) "`x ∈ Ann N` annihilates `Ext^i(N, M)`"; this
351	    -- is the algebraic fact under both directions of the iff. The remaining
352	    -- pieces are LES-of-Ext bookkeeping + supremum-extraction.
353	    --
354	    -- The remaining substantive Mathlib gaps for closing this step are:
355	    --   (b) Extracting `Module.annihilator R (ResidueField R) = maximalIdeal R`
356	    --       (a one-liner via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`).
357	    --   (c) The "sSup gives a witness of length ≥ n+1" reasoning, which
358	    --       in `ℕ∞` requires handling the `⊤` case (Nakayama rules it out
359	    --       under `Nontrivial M`).
360	    --   (d) Lifting `IsSMulRegular`-cons via `isRegular_cons_iff`.
361	    --
362	    -- We open the backward direction's regular-element-extraction step
363	    -- below to expose the API path for the next iter; the forward
364	    -- direction's regular-sequence-extraction + LES chase is the larger
365	    -- body remaining as `sorry`.
366	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
367	    · -- (⇒) Forward direction: `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M → ∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0`.
368	      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via Nakayama-driven `depth = sSup`
369	      -- extraction + cons-decomposition + LES chase using
370	      -- `ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator` + `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
371	      intro _hdepth i _hi _e
372	      -- Step 1: unfold `depth M = sSup S_M` (Nakayama rules out `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤`).
373	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
374	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
375	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
376	      have hdepth_M_unfold :
377	          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
378	            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
379	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
380	              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
381	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
382	      -- Step 2: extract a regular sequence rs of length > n in 𝔪 on M.
383	      have hlt : (n : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
384	        calc (n : ℕ∞)
385	            < ((n + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.lt_succ_self n
386	          _ ≤ _ := _hdepth
387	      rw [hdepth_M_unfold] at hlt
388	      obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
389	      have hrs_len_gt : n < rs.length := by
390	        rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
391	        exact_mod_cast hk_lt
392	      -- Step 3: rs is non-empty (length > n ≥ 0); decompose rs = x :: rs_tail.
393	      rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
394	      · -- impossible: empty list has length 0, not > n.
395	        simp at hrs_len_gt
396	      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self
397	      have htail_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_tail, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
398	        hrs_mem r (List.mem_cons_of_mem _ hr)
399	      have hcons := (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg
400	      have hxReg : IsSMulRegular M x := hcons.1
401	      have hrs_tail_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular (QuotSMulTop x M) rs_tail :=
402	        hcons.2
403	      have htail_len_ge : n ≤ rs_tail.length := by
404	        have h1 : n < (x :: rs_tail).length := hrs_len_gt
405	        simp [List.length_cons] at h1
406	        omega
407	      -- Step 4: `x ∈ Ann(κ)` via `Ideal.annihilator_quotient`.
408	      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
409	          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := Ideal.annihilator_quotient
410	      have hxAnnih : x ∈ Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) :=
411	        hannih ▸ hxMem
412	      -- Step 5: build MxM and show depth MxM ≥ n via the prefix rs_tail.take n.
413	      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
414	      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
415	        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
416	      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
417	      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
418	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
419	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
420	      let rs_n := rs_tail.take n
421	      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
422	        change (rs_tail.take n).length = n
423	        rw [List.length_take]
424	        omega
425	      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
426	        htail_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
427	      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
428	        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n)
429	        have hsplit : rs_tail = rs_tail.take n ++ rs_tail.drop n :=
430	          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
431	        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs_tail :=
432	          hrs_tail_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
433	        rw [hsplit] at hwr
434	        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs_tail.take n) :=
435	          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
436	        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
437	          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
438	      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
439	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
440	        apply le_sSup
441	        refine ⟨rs_n, ?_, hrs_n_mem, hrs_n_reg⟩
442	        exact_mod_cast hrs_n_len
443	      -- Step 6: apply ih (M := MxM) at index n.
444	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
445	          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
446	          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 :=
447	        (ih (M := MxM)).mp hdepth_MxM
448	      -- Step 7: LES chase. Set up SES.
449	      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
450	        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
451	      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
452	      -- `S.f = x • 𝟙_M` (definitional), hence `mk₀ S.f = x • mk₀ 𝟙`, hence
453	      -- `_e.comp (mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e = 0` (by helper at `x ∈ Ann κ`).
454	      have hSf_eq_smul : S.f = x • (𝟙 (ModuleCat.of R M) : _ ⟶ _) := rfl
455	      have hSf_kill :
456	          _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 := by
457	        have hcomp :
458	            _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = x • _e := by
459	          rw [hSf_eq_smul]
460	          exact (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.smul_eq_comp_mk₀ _e x).symm
461	        rw [hcomp]
462	        exact ext_smul_eq_zero_of_mem_annihilator _e hxAnnih
463	      -- Split on i = 0 vs i ≥ 1.
464	      rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi_pos
465	      · subst hi0
466	        -- Use mono S.f (since hxReg) + postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono.
467	        haveI hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f := by
468	          rw [ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective]
469	          exact hxReg
470	        have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono
471	          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) S.f
472	        apply hinj
473	        change _e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero 0)
474	          = (0 : Abelian.Ext.{u} (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
475	              (ModuleCat.of R M) 0).comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f)
476	              (add_zero 0)
477	        rw [CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.zero_comp]
478	        exact hSf_kill
479	      · -- i ≥ 1: write i = j + 1, then j < n and use covariant_sequence_exact₁.
480	        obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero
481	          (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi_pos)
482	        have hj : j < n := by omega
483	        obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
484	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁
485	            (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)) hS _e hSf_kill rfl
486	        have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := hMxM_vanish j hj _
487	        rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
488	        simpa using hx₃.symm
489	    · -- (⇐) Backward direction: `(∀ i ≤ n, Ext^i(κ, M) = 0) → (n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M`.
490	      intro hext
491	      -- Step 1: From Ext^0(κ, M) = 0, extract `Subsingleton (κ →ₗ[R] M)`.
492	      -- For all R-linear maps `f g : κ →ₗ[R] M`, `mk₀ (ofHom f) = 0` in Ext^0
493	      -- (by `hext 0`), so via `mk₀_eq_zero_iff` the morphism `ofHom f = 0`,
494	      -- hence `f = 0`.  Both `f = 0 = g`.
495	      have hext0 : ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u}
496	          (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
497	          (ModuleCat.of R M) 0, e = 0 := hext 0 (Nat.succ_pos n)
498	      have hsubsing : Subsingleton (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R →ₗ[R] M) := by
499	        refine ⟨fun f g => ?_⟩
500	        have hf : (ModuleCat.ofHom f : ModuleCat.of R _ ⟶ ModuleCat.of R M) = 0 :=
501	          (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_eq_zero_iff (ModuleCat.ofHom f)).mp
502	            (hext0 _)
503	        have hg : (ModuleCat.ofHom g : ModuleCat.of R _ ⟶ ModuleCat.of R M) = 0 :=
504	          (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀_eq_zero_iff (ModuleCat.ofHom g)).mp
505	            (hext0 _)
506	        have hf' : f = 0 := ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff.mp hf
507	        have hg' : g = 0 := ModuleCat.hom_ext_iff.mp hg
508	        rw [hf', hg']
509	      -- Step 2: Apply `subsingleton_linearMap_iff` to extract a regular element.
510	      -- The annihilator of `R ⧸ maximalIdeal R = ResidueField R` is `maximalIdeal R`
511	      -- by `Ideal.annihilator_quotient` (under the two-sided instance — automatic
512	      -- for commutative R).
513	      have hannih : Module.annihilator R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) =
514	          IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R :=
515	        Ideal.annihilator_quotient
516	      have ⟨x, hxAnnih, hxReg⟩ :=
517	        IsSMulRegular.subsingleton_linearMap_iff.mp hsubsing
518	      have hxMem : x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := hannih ▸ hxAnnih
519	      -- Step 3: Pass to `M/xM := QuotSMulTop x M` and apply IH at index n.
520	      -- iter-184+: the remaining LES chase to derive Ext-vanishing on M/xM,
521	      -- application of IH, and `isRegular_cons_iff` assembly closes the rest.
522	      -- We package the partial state in named witnesses for the next iter.
523	      let MxM : Type u := QuotSMulTop x M
524	      haveI : Nontrivial MxM :=
525	        nontrivial_quotSMulTop_of_mem_maximalIdeal M hxMem
526	      -- `Module.Finite R (M / xM)` is automatic via `Module.Finite.quotient`.
527	      haveI : _root_.Module.Finite R MxM := inferInstance
528	      -- iter-184 Lane G: closed via LES chase + sSup extraction + cons.
529	      --
530	      -- Step A: derive `∀ j < n, Ext^j(κ, MxM) = 0` from `hext` via the
531	      --   LES of `Ext^*(κ, ·)` on the SES `0 → M →[x] M → MxM → 0`.
532	      let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
533	        ModuleCat.smulShortComplex (ModuleCat.of R M) x
534	      have hS : S.ShortExact := hxReg.smulShortComplex_shortExact
535	      set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
536	        with hκ
537	      have hMxM_vanish : ∀ j < n, ∀ e : Abelian.Ext.{u} κ
538	          (ModuleCat.of R MxM) j, e = 0 := by
539	        intro j hj e
540	        -- `e.comp hS.extClass rfl : Ext κ M (j+1) = 0` by `hext` at `j+1`.
541	        have he_ext : e.comp hS.extClass (rfl : j + 1 = j + 1) = 0 :=
542	          hext (j + 1) (by omega) _
543	        obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
544	          CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl he_ext
545	        -- `x₂ : Ext κ M j = 0` by `hext` at `j`.
546	        have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := hext j (by omega) _
547	        rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
548	        simpa using hx₂.symm
549	      -- Step B: apply `ih (M := MxM)` at index `n`.
550	      have hdepth_MxM : (n : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM :=
551	        (ih (M := MxM)).mpr hMxM_vanish
552	      -- Step C: unfold `depth MxM = sSup`-clause and extract a witness rs'
553	      --   of length ≥ n on MxM in 𝔪 (when n ≥ 1; the n = 0 case uses []).
554	      have hne_MxM : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R MxM) ≠ ⊤ :=
555	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
556	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
557	      have hdepth_MxM_unfold :
558	          depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM
559	            = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
560	              (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
561	              ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs } := by
562	        rw [depth, if_neg hne_MxM]
563	      obtain ⟨rs', hrs'_len_ge, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩ :
564	          ∃ rs' : List R, n ≤ rs'.length ∧
565	            (∀ r ∈ rs', r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) ∧
566	            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs' := by
567	        rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos n with hn0 | hn_pos
568	        · subst hn0
569	          exact ⟨[], by simp, by simp,
570	            RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular.nil R MxM⟩
571	        · have hlt : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) <
572	              depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) MxM := by
573	            have h1 : ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < (n : ℕ∞) := by
574	              exact_mod_cast Nat.sub_lt hn_pos Nat.one_pos
575	            exact h1.trans_le hdepth_MxM
576	          rw [hdepth_MxM_unfold] at hlt
577	          obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs', hrs'_len_eq, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ :=
578	            lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
579	          have hrs'_len_gt : n - 1 < rs'.length := by
580	            rw [← hrs'_len_eq] at hk_lt
581	            exact_mod_cast hk_lt
582	          refine ⟨rs', ?_, hrs'_mem, hrs'_reg⟩
583	          omega
584	      -- Step D: truncate rs' to length n; resulting sequence is M-regular.
585	      let rs_n := rs'.take n
586	      have hrs_n_len : rs_n.length = n := by
587	        change (rs'.take n).length = n
588	        rw [List.length_take]
589	        omega
590	      have hrs_n_mem : ∀ r ∈ rs_n, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := fun r hr =>
591	        hrs'_mem r (List.mem_of_mem_take hr)
592	      have hrs_n_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM rs_n := by
593	        change RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular MxM (rs'.take n)
594	        have hsplit : rs' = rs'.take n ++ rs'.drop n :=
595	          (List.take_append_drop _ _).symm
596	        have hwr : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM rs' :=
597	          hrs'_reg.toIsWeaklyRegular
598	        rw [hsplit] at hwr
599	        have hwr_n : RingTheory.Sequence.IsWeaklyRegular MxM (rs'.take n) :=
600	          ((RingTheory.Sequence.isWeaklyRegular_append_iff MxM _ _).mp hwr).1
601	        exact (IsLocalRing.isRegular_iff_isWeaklyRegular_of_subset_maximalIdeal
602	          hrs_n_mem).mpr hwr_n
603	      -- Step E: cons x to form a length-(n+1) M-regular sequence in 𝔪.
604	      have hrs_M_reg : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M (x :: rs_n) :=
605	        (RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_n).mpr ⟨hxReg, hrs_n_reg⟩
606	      have hrs_M_mem : ∀ r ∈ (x :: rs_n), r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
607	        intro r hr
608	        rcases List.mem_cons.mp hr with rfl | hr
609	        · exact hxMem
610	        · exact hrs_n_mem r hr
611	      have hrs_M_len : (x :: rs_n).length = n + 1 := by simp [hrs_n_len]
612	      -- Step F: conclude `(n+1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth M` via `le_sSup`.
613	      have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
614	        Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
615	          (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
616	      rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
617	      apply le_sSup
618	      refine ⟨x :: rs_n, ?_, hrs_M_mem, hrs_M_reg⟩
619	      exact_mod_cast hrs_M_len
620	
621	/-! ## §4. Depth on a short exact sequence
622	
623	For a short exact sequence `0 → N' → N → N'' → 0` of nonzero finite modules
624	over a Noetherian local ring, the three modules' depths satisfy three
625	crosswise inequalities (Stacks tag 00LE), each a direct read-off of the
626	long exact `Ext^*(κ, -)` sequence and the depth-via-Ext characterisation
627	of §3.
628	
629	Blueprint reference: `lem:depth_short_exact_sequence` (Stacks tag 00LE). -/
630	
631	/-! ### Helper A (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): Ext-vanishing from strict depth bound
632	
633	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`,
634	if `(i : ℕ∞) < depth M` then every element of `Ext^i_R(κ, M)` is zero.
635	
636	This packages `depth_eq_smallest_ext_index` for the LES chase: the
637	`n ≤ depth M` form with `n := i + 1` instantiates the `∀ j < i + 1`
638	quantifier at `j = i`. Body is kernel-clean modulo the typed sorry of
639	`depth_eq_smallest_ext_index`. -/
640	private lemma ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth
641	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
642	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
643	    [Nontrivial M] {i : ℕ}
644	    (h : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M)
645	    (e : CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.{u}
646	        (ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R))
647	        (ModuleCat.of R M) i) : e = 0 := by
648	  have h' : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M := by
649	    have hcast : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (i : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
650	    rw [hcast]; exact Order.add_one_le_of_lt h
651	  exact (depth_eq_smallest_ext_index (M := M) (i + 1)).mp h' i (Nat.lt_succ_self i) e
652	
653	/-! ### Helper B (iter-182 Lane G, axiom-clean): `ℕ∞` tsub bridge
654	
655	If `(a : ℕ) ≤ d - 1` in `ℕ∞` and `1 ≤ a` (in `ℕ`), then
656	`((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d`.
657	
658	Case-split on `d = ⊤` (trivial) and `d = ↑n` (drop to `ℕ` arithmetic).
659	Used for the `depth N' - 1` shift in the second SES inequality. -/
660	private lemma natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one
661	    {d : ℕ∞} {a : ℕ} (ha : 1 ≤ a) (h : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ d - 1) :
662	    ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ d := by
663	  rcases eq_or_ne d ⊤ with hd | hd
664	  · simp [hd]
665	  · obtain ⟨n, rfl⟩ := WithTop.ne_top_iff_exists.mp hd
666	    -- Reduce to ℕ: turn `↑a ≤ ↑n - 1` into `a ≤ n - 1`, then `a + 1 ≤ n`.
667	    have h₂ : (a : ℕ∞) ≤ ((n - 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by
668	      refine h.trans (le_of_eq ?_)
669	      rcases n with _ | n
670	      · rfl
671	      · push_cast; rfl
672	    have han : a ≤ n - 1 := by exact_mod_cast h₂
673	    have hle : a + 1 ≤ n := by omega
674	    exact Nat.cast_le.mpr hle
675	
676	theorem depth_of_short_exact
677	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
678	    {N' N N'' : Type u}
679	    [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N'] [_root_.Module.Finite R N'] [Nontrivial N']
680	    [AddCommGroup N] [Module R N] [_root_.Module.Finite R N] [Nontrivial N]
681	    [AddCommGroup N''] [Module R N''] [_root_.Module.Finite R N''] [Nontrivial N'']
682	    (f : N' →ₗ[R] N) (g : N →ₗ[R] N'')
683	    (_hf : Function.Injective f) (_hg : Function.Surjective g)
684	    (_hex : Function.Exact f g) :
685	    min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N')
686	        (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'')
687	      ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N
688	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
689	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' - 1)
690	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N''
691	    ∧ min (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N)
692	          (depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1)
693	        ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
694	  -- Package the SES as a `ShortComplex.ShortExact` in `ModuleCat.{u} R`.
695	  let S : ShortComplex (ModuleCat.{u} R) :=
696	    ShortComplex.mk (ModuleCat.ofHom f) (ModuleCat.ofHom g)
697	      (by ext x; simpa using _hex.apply_apply_eq_zero x)
698	  have hS : S.ShortExact :=
699	    ModuleCat.shortComplex_shortExact S _hex _hf _hg
700	  -- The residue field as a ModuleCat object.
701	  set κ : ModuleCat.{u} R := ModuleCat.of R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) with hκ
702	  refine ⟨?_, ?_, ?_⟩
703	  · -- (1) min(depth N', depth N'') ≤ depth N
704	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
705	    intro a ha
706	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
707	    obtain ⟨haN', haN''⟩ := ha
708	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
709	    intro i hi e
710	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₂ i = Ext κ (of R N) i`; goal `e = 0`.
711	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
712	    have hiN' : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := hicast.trans_le haN'
713	    have hiN'' : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := hicast.trans_le haN''
714	    -- `e ∘ S.g ∈ Ext κ (of R N'') i = 0`.
715	    have heg : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.g) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
716	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN'' _
717	    obtain ⟨x₁, hx₁⟩ :=
718	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₂ κ hS e heg
719	    -- `x₁ ∈ Ext κ (of R N') i = 0`.
720	    have hx₁_zero : x₁ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN' _
721	    rw [hx₁_zero] at hx₁
722	    simpa using hx₁.symm
723	  · -- (2) min(depth N, depth N' - 1) ≤ depth N''
724	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
725	    intro a ha
726	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
727	    obtain ⟨haN, haN'sub⟩ := ha
728	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
729	    intro i hi e
730	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₃ i = Ext κ (of R N'') i`; goal `e = 0`.
731	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
732	    have hiN : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
733	    -- `↑(i+1) < depth N'`: use Helper B with `a` and the inequality `hi : i + 1 ≤ a`.
734	    have hia : 1 ≤ a := by omega
735	    have ha1 : ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' :=
736	      natCast_add_one_le_of_le_sub_one hia haN'sub
737	    have hsucc : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N' := by
738	      have : ((i + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) < ((a + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast Nat.add_lt_add_right hi 1
739	      exact this.trans_le ha1
740	    -- `e ∘ extClass ∈ Ext κ (of R N') (i + 1) = 0`.
741	    have hext : e.comp hS.extClass rfl = 0 :=
742	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hsucc _
743	    obtain ⟨x₂, hx₂⟩ :=
744	      CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₃ κ hS e rfl hext
745	    -- `x₂ ∈ Ext κ (of R N) i = 0`.
746	    have hx₂_zero : x₂ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
747	    rw [hx₂_zero] at hx₂
748	    simpa using hx₂.symm
749	  · -- (3) min(depth N, depth N'' + 1) ≤ depth N'
750	    rw [← ENat.forall_natCast_le_iff_le]
751	    intro a ha
752	    rw [le_min_iff] at ha
753	    obtain ⟨haN, haN''add⟩ := ha
754	    rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]
755	    intro i hi e
756	    -- `e : Ext κ S.X₁ i = Ext κ (of R N') i`; goal `e = 0`.
757	    have hicast : (i : ℕ∞) < (a : ℕ∞) := by exact_mod_cast hi
758	    have hiN : (i : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N := hicast.trans_le haN
759	    -- `e ∘ S.f ∈ Ext κ (of R N) i = 0`.
760	    have hef : e.comp (CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.mk₀ S.f) (add_zero i) = 0 :=
761	      ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hiN _
762	    -- Split on `i = 0` vs `i ≥ 1`. For `i ≥ 1`, use `covariant_sequence_exact₁`.
763	    -- For `i = 0`, postcomposition by `S.f` is injective (since `S.f` is mono).
764	    rcases Nat.eq_zero_or_pos i with hi0 | hi0
765	    · subst hi0
766	      -- `e : Ext κ S.X₁ 0`; postcomp by `S.f` is injective; image is `e ∘ S.f = 0`,
767	      -- so `e = 0`.
768	      have hmono : CategoryTheory.Mono S.f :=
769	        (ModuleCat.mono_iff_injective _).mpr _hf
770	      have hinj := CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.postcomp_mk₀_injective_of_mono κ S.f
771	      apply hinj
772	      simpa using hef
773	    · -- `i ≥ 1`. Let `i = j + 1` and use `covariant_sequence_exact₁` at
774	      -- `n₀ = j, n₁ = i = j + 1`.
775	      obtain ⟨j, rfl⟩ := Nat.exists_eq_succ_of_ne_zero (Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mp hi0)
776	      -- `e : Ext κ (of R N') (j + 1)`. We need `Ext κ (of R N'') j = 0`.
777	      -- From `↑(j+2) ≤ ↑a ≤ depth N'' + 1`, get `↑j + 1 ≤ depth N''`, so `↑j < depth N''`.
778	      have hjN'' : (j : ℕ∞) < depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' := by
779	        have hja : j + 2 ≤ a := by omega
780	        have h_j2 : ((j + 2 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' + 1 := by
781	          refine le_trans ?_ haN''add
782	          exact_mod_cast hja
783	        have hcast : ((j + 2 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
784	        rw [hcast] at h_j2
785	        have h_canc : ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) N'' :=
786	          (ENat.add_le_add_iff_right (by norm_num : (1 : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)).mp h_j2
787	        have hcast2 : ((j + 1 : ℕ) : ℕ∞) = (j : ℕ∞) + 1 := by push_cast; ring
788	        rw [hcast2] at h_canc
789	        exact (ENat.add_one_le_iff (by simp : (j : ℕ∞) ≠ ⊤)).mp h_canc
790	      obtain ⟨x₃, hx₃⟩ :=
791	        CategoryTheory.Abelian.Ext.covariant_sequence_exact₁ κ hS e hef rfl
792	      -- `x₃ ∈ Ext κ (of R N'') j = 0`.
793	      have hx₃_zero : x₃ = 0 := ext_vanish_of_natCast_lt_depth hjN'' _
794	      rw [hx₃_zero] at hx₃
795	      simpa using hx₃.symm
796	
797	/-! ### Helper iter-193 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth is preserved under R-linear equivalence
798	
799	For a commutative ring `R`, an ideal `I ⊆ R`, and two `R`-modules `M, M'` with
800	an `R`-linear equivalence `e : M ≃ₗ[R] M'`, we have `depth I M = depth I M'`.
801	
802	This is the standard "depth is an invariant of the isomorphism class" fact;
803	the proof has two steps: (1) the side-condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved
804	under linear equivalence, and (2) the regular-sequence supremum sets agree
805	via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`. Both halves are direct Mathlib calls; the
806	helper is axiom-clean.
807	
808	iter-193 Lane G structural advance: this helper is the substrate piece
809	needed to identify `depth(M)` with `depth(R^k)` for `M` finite free, which
810	in turn closes the `pd(M) = 0` base case of the Auslander–Buchsbaum
811	formula modulo the `depth(R^k) = depth(R)` direct-sum step (a separate
812	substrate item; the file's residual sorries are narrowed to that one
813	named piece). -/
814	lemma depth_eq_of_linearEquiv {R : Type u} [CommRing R] (I : Ideal R)
815	    {M M' : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [AddCommGroup M'] [Module R M']
816	    (e : M ≃ₗ[R] M') :
817	    depth I M = depth I M' := by
818	  -- Step 1: `I • ⊤ = ⊤` is preserved under the linear equivalence.
819	  have hcond : (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤) ↔ (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤) := by
820	    have e_top : Submodule.map (e : M →ₗ[R] M') (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
821	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e
822	    have e_symm_top :
823	        Submodule.map (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M) (⊤ : Submodule R M') = ⊤ := by
824	      rw [Submodule.map_top]; exact LinearEquiv.range e.symm
825	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
826	    · intro h
827	      have hmap :=
828	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M) (e : M →ₗ[R] M')
829	      rw [h, e_top] at hmap
830	      exact hmap.symm
831	    · intro h
832	      have hmap :=
833	        Submodule.map_smul'' I (⊤ : Submodule R M') (e.symm : M' →ₗ[R] M)
834	      rw [h, e_symm_top] at hmap
835	      exact hmap.symm
836	  -- Step 2: the `sSup` sets agree via `LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr`.
837	  unfold depth
838	  by_cases h : I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤
839	  · simp [if_pos h, if_pos (hcond.mp h)]
840	  · rw [if_neg h, if_neg (mt hcond.mpr h)]
841	    congr 1
842	    ext n
843	    refine ⟨?_, ?_⟩
844	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
845	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mp hreg⟩
846	    · rintro ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, hreg⟩
847	      exact ⟨rs, hlen, hmem, (LinearEquiv.isRegular_congr e rs).mpr hreg⟩
848	
849	/-! ### Helper iter-194 Lane G (axiom-clean): depth of constant Pi equals depth of fiber.
850	
851	For a commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, module `M`, and nonempty finite type `ι`,
852	`depth I (ι → M) = depth I M`. The proof goes through the regular-sequence
853	characterization: each `r`-action on `ι → M` is pointwise (so an `r ∈ R` is
854	regular on `ι → M` iff regular on `M`), and the quotient `(ι → M)/r·⊤`
855	identifies with `ι → M/r·⊤` via `Submodule.quotientPi`. The side condition
856	`I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on both sides via a `Pi.single` lifting argument.
857	
858	This is the substrate needed to close the `pd_R(M) = 0` case of the
859	Auslander–Buchsbaum formula (where `M ≃ₗ[R] Fin k → R` via a basis). -/
860	
861	/-- For any commutative ring `R`, ideal `I`, finite index `ι`, and module `M`,
862	the ideal-action `I • ⊤_{ι → M}` equals the pi-submodule of fibre `I • ⊤_M`s. -/
863	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const
864	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι]
865	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
866	    (I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))) =
867	      Submodule.pi (Set.univ : Set ι) (fun (_ : ι) => I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) := by
868	  apply le_antisymm
869	  · intro f hf i _
870	    refine Submodule.smul_induction_on hf ?_ ?_
871	    · intro a hain x _
872	      change a • x i ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)
873	      exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul hain trivial
874	    · intro x y hx hy
875	      change (x + y) i ∈ _
876	      exact Submodule.add_mem _ hx hy
877	  · intro f hf
878	    rw [show f = ∑ j, Pi.single j (f j) from (Finset.univ_sum_single f).symm]
879	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
880	    intro j _
881	    have hfj : f j ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) := hf j (Set.mem_univ j)
882	    have hmap :
883	        Pi.single j (f j) ∈
884	          Submodule.map (LinearMap.single R (fun (_ : ι) => M) j)
885	            (I • (⊤ : Submodule R M)) :=
886	      Submodule.mem_map.mpr ⟨f j, hfj, rfl⟩
887	    rw [Submodule.map_smul''] at hmap
888	    exact Submodule.smul_mono le_rfl le_top hmap
889	
890	/-- The side condition `I • ⊤ = ⊤` agrees on `ι → M` and `M` for nonempty
891	finite `ι`: a free product of fibre `I•⊤_M`-witnesses combines to a
892	`I•⊤_{ι → M}`-witness (via `Pi.single`-lifting), and conversely a
893	`Pi.single j m`-projection at `j` reads off the witness on the fibre. -/
894	private lemma ideal_smul_top_pi_const_eq_top_iff
895	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] [Nonempty ι]
896	    (I : Ideal R) {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
897	    I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) = ⊤ ↔ I • (⊤ : Submodule R M) = ⊤ := by
898	  constructor
899	  · intro h
900	    rw [eq_top_iff]
901	    intro m _
902	    obtain ⟨j⟩ := ‹Nonempty ι›
903	    have hsingle_mem :
904	        (Pi.single j m : ι → M) ∈ I • (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M)) := by
905	      rw [h]; trivial
906	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const] at hsingle_mem
907	    have := hsingle_mem j (Set.mem_univ j)
908	    rwa [Pi.single_eq_same] at this
909	  · intro h
910	    rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const, eq_top_iff]
911	    intro f _ i _
912	    rw [h]
913	    trivial
914	
915	/-- `QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] ι → QuotSMulTop r M` for finite `ι`,
916	obtained by rewriting `r • ⊤ = Ideal.span {r} • ⊤` and using
917	`Submodule.quotientPi`. -/
918	private noncomputable def quotSMulTopPiConstLinearEquiv
919	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] {ι : Type*} [Fintype ι] [DecidableEq ι] (r : R)
920	    {M : Type v} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] :
921	    QuotSMulTop r (ι → M) ≃ₗ[R] (ι → QuotSMulTop r M) := by
922	  refine (Submodule.quotEquivOfEq _ _ ?_).trans (Submodule.quotientPi _)
923	  rw [← Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r (⊤ : Submodule R (ι → M))]
924	  rw [ideal_smul_top_pi_const]
925	  congr 1
926	  funext _
927	  exact Submodule.ideal_span_singleton_smul r ⊤
928	
929	/-- For nonempty finite `ι`, a list `rs : List R` is `(ι → M)`-regular iff it
930	is `M`-regular. Proof by induction on `rs`: the empty case reduces to
931	`Nontrivial (ι → M) ↔ Nontrivial M`; the cons case uses `Pi.isSMulRegular_iff`

10	/-!
11	# Codimension-1 indeterminacy extension (A.4.a)
12	
13	This file is the **A.4.a** sub-build chapter for the positive-genus arm of the
14	project's `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It packages the two extension inputs to
15	Milne's *Abelian Varieties* §I.3 Theorem 3.2, plus the Weil-divisor
16	reformulation of the codim-`1` obstruction:
17	
18	* **Milne Theorem 3.1** (`extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`): a rational map
19	  from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has indeterminacy locus
20	  of codimension `≥ 2`; if additionally the map is already
21	  codim-`1`-indeterminacy-free, then it extends to a regular morphism.
22	* **Milne Lemma 3.3** (`indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`): for a
23	  rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety, the
24	  indeterminacy locus is either empty or of pure codimension `1`.
25	* **Weil-divisor obstruction** (`mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`):
26	  `f` is defined at the generic point of a prime divisor `W` iff some
27	  `PartialMap` representative of `f` is regular at `W.point`. This is a thin
28	  re-shuffle of Mathlib's `RationalMap.mem_domain`; the substantive
29	  Hartshorne-II.6 "regular ⇔ order ≥ 0 at every DVR pullback" reformulation
30	  (the original Weil-divisor obstruction) is deferred to a future lemma
31	  that builds genuine pullback machinery (`Scheme.RationalMap.order`-along-`f`),
32	  outside this iter's scope.
33	
34	These outputs feed Milne Theorem 3.2 in the sibling
35	`Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean` (A.4.c) and consume
36	`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (A.4.b)
37	at Step 2 of `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth`.
38	
39	## Status (iter-177 Lane 6 file-skeleton)
40	
41	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended*
42	substantive type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
43	`blueprint/src/chapters/Albanese_CodimOneExtension.tex`) with a `sorry` body.
44	Bodies are iter-178+ work, gated on the local-cohomology / depth-≥2
45	extension lemma (Step 2 of `thm:codim_one_extension`) being supplied either
46	from `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (the `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`
47	input) or from a Mathlib upstream once the local-cohomology vanishing
48	`H¹_{x}(V, 𝒪_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point lands.
49	
50	The 6 pinned declarations are:
51	
52	1. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacyLocus` (def, ~3 LOC) — the closed
53	   complement of `RationalMap.domain` as a `Set X`.
54	2. `Scheme.RationalMap.CodimOneFree` (def, ~3 LOC) — predicate: every
55	   point `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in `f.domain`.
56	3. `Scheme.localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` (theorem, ~3 LOC) — for a smooth
57	   integral variety, the stalk at a codim-1 point is a DVR.
58	4. `Scheme.RationalMap.extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` (theorem, ~10 LOC) —
59	   Milne 3.1 specialised to smooth source + complete target +
60	   codim-1-indeterminacy-free.
61	5. `Scheme.RationalMap.indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme`
62	   (theorem, ~8 LOC) — Milne Lemma 3.3.
63	6. `Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point`
64	   (theorem, ~2 LOC) — definitional re-shuffle of `RationalMap.mem_domain`
65	   exposing a `PartialMap` representative whose domain contains `W.point`.
66	   The substantive Weil-divisor `order ≥ 0` reformulation is deferred (see
67	   header).
68	
69	## Note on type expressivity
70	
71	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
72	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
73	
74	* `indeterminacyLocus f : Set X` returns the carrier of the closed
75	  complement of `f.domain` — not `∅` or `Set.univ`.
76	* `CodimOneFree f : Prop` is the genuine ∀-statement over codim-1 points,
77	  not `True`.
78	* `localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` produces a Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing`
79	  instance — not a trivial proposition.
80	* `extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth` asserts `∃! g, g.toRationalMap = f`
81	  — the existence of a unique regular extension.
82	* `indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme` asserts a disjunction:
83	  either the locus is empty or every component has coheight `1`.
84	* `mem_domain_iff_exists_partialMap_through_point` asserts the (truthful)
85	  iff between definedness at `W.point` and the existence of a `PartialMap`
86	  representative containing `W.point` in its domain — a definitional unfold
87	  of `Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap.mem_domain` with the
88	  conjunction swapped for downstream ergonomics.
89	
90	Unfolding any pinned declaration exposes the named substantive content
91	(the carrier set complement, the `∀ η, ...` statement, the DVR instance,
92	the unique regular extension, …); no `Iso.refl _` / empty-content
93	`proof_wanted` / `Classical.choice ⟨witness⟩` placeholders are used.
94	
95	## Variety conventions
96	
97	Following the project (cf. `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`,
98	`AbelianVarietyRigidity.lean`), a **nonsingular variety over `k̄`** is an
99	object `X : Over (Spec (.of k̄))` carrying:
100	
101	* `[Smooth X.hom]`, `[GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]`,
102	* `[IsSeparated X.hom]`, `[LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]`,
103	* `[IsIntegral X.left]`, `[IsReduced X.left]`.
104	
105	A **complete variety** adds `[IsProper Y.hom]`. A **group variety** over
106	`k̄` adds a `[GrpObj Y]` instance to the variety package.
107

112	Lemma 3.3 (p. 17); Hartshorne, *Algebraic Geometry*, II.6 pp. 130–131.
113	-/

127	/-! ## §1. The indeterminacy locus of a rational map
128	
129	The indeterminacy locus `Z(f) ⊆ X` of a rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is the closed
130	complement of its domain of definition `Dom(f) := f.domain` (a Mathlib-shipped
131	open subset of `X`).
132	
133	Blueprint pin: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne §I.3 p. 16). -/
134	
135	/-- **Indeterminacy locus of a rational map.** The closed complement of the
136	domain of definition `f.domain` (an `X.Opens`, exposed by Mathlib at
137	`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Birational.RationalMap`):
138	
139	`Z(f) := X ∖ Dom(f) ⊆ X`.
140	
141	`f` is everywhere defined (i.e. extends to a regular morphism) iff
142	`indeterminacyLocus f = ∅`.
143	
144	Blueprint reference: `def:indeterminacy_locus` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
145	§I.3, p. 16). -/
146	def indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Set X :=
147	  (f.domain : Set X)ᶜ
148	
149	/-- The indeterminacy locus of a rational map is closed (it is the complement
150	of an open). -/
151	lemma isClosed_indeterminacyLocus {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) :
152	    IsClosed (indeterminacyLocus f) := by
153	  unfold indeterminacyLocus
154	  exact (f.domain.isOpen).isClosed_compl
155	
156	/-! ## §2. Codimension-1-indeterminacy-free rational maps
157	
158	A rational map `f : X ⇢ Y` is **codim-1-indeterminacy-free** if its
159	indeterminacy locus contains no prime divisor of `X`, i.e. every codim-1
160	generic point of `X` already lies in `f.domain`. Equivalently (under the
161	project's standing `Order.coheight` convention on the specialisation
162	preorder of `X.carrier`), every `η : X` with `Order.coheight η = 1` lies in
163	`f.domain`.
164	
165	Blueprint pin: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1, the
166	conclusion). -/
167	
168	/-- **Codim-1-indeterminacy-free rational map.** A rational map
169	`f : X ⇢ Y` is codim-1-indeterminacy-free if every codimension-one point
170	of `X` lies in its domain of definition. Equivalently: no prime divisor of
171	`X` (i.e. closure of a codim-1 generic point) is contained in
172	`indeterminacyLocus f`.
173	
174	The codim-1 condition is encoded via `Order.coheight η = 1` on the
175	specialisation preorder of `X.carrier`, matching the project's standing
176	convention in `Scheme.PrimeDivisor` (`RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`).
177	
178	Blueprint reference: `def:codim_one_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
179	Varieties*, Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
180	def CodimOneFree {X Y : Scheme.{u}} (f : X.RationalMap Y) : Prop :=
181	  ∀ (x : X), Order.coheight x = 1 → x ∈ f.domain
182	
183	end RationalMap
184	
185	/-! ## §3. Smoothness yields a DVR at every codim-1 point
186	
187	On a nonsingular variety `X` over `k̄`, the local ring at the generic point
188	of a prime divisor is a discrete valuation ring with fraction field equal to
189	the function field `K(X) = X.functionField`. This is Hartshorne II.6's
190	"regular in codimension one" property combined with the regular-local-ring-of-dim-1
191	$\Leftrightarrow$ DVR equivalence (Atiyah–Macdonald 9.2; Stacks `00PD`).
192	
193	Blueprint pin: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130). -/
194	
195	/-! ### Stacks 00TT scaffolding (Lane M↓ iter-191 first scaffold)
196	
197	The Mathlib gap "smooth over `\bar k` ⟹ stalk is a regular local ring"
198	(Stacks tag `00TT`) is structured here as a four-stage pipeline:
199	
200	* **Stage 1** — smooth ⟹ flat at every stalk (Mathlib instance
201	  `AlgebraicGeometry.instFlatOfSmooth` + `Flat.stalkMap`).
202	* **Stage 2** — smooth at a point ⟹ exists a standard smooth presentation
203	  on an affine neighbourhood (Stacks tag `00T7`, packaged in Mathlib at
204	  `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`).
205	* **Stage 3** — the polynomial generators of the standard smooth
206	  presentation form a regular sequence after localising at any prime
207	  (Stacks `02JK`/`07BV`; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
208	* **Stage 4** — a Noetherian local ring whose maximal ideal is generated
209	  by a regular sequence of length equal to its Krull dimension is a
210	  regular local ring (`IsRegularLocalRing.of_regularSequence`; Stacks
211	  `00OE`/Matsumura 19.2; Mathlib gap as of `b80f227`).
212	
213	Stages 1-2 are landed axiom-clean as named helpers below. Stages 3-4
214	remain a single scoped `sorry` inside the main theorem
215	`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth` until either the cotangent /
216	sheaf-of-relative-differentials route (Mathlib upstream) lands or the
217	project-side `02JK`/`00OE` chain is built (iter-192+). -/
218	
219	/-- **Stage 1 (Stacks 00TT, smooth ⟹ flat at stalk).** For a smooth
220	morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)` with `k̄` algebraically closed,
221	the induced ring map on stalks at every point `z : X.left` is flat. This
222	is the smooth ⟹ flat instance applied through the stalk-map flatness
223	characterisation `AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.iff_flat_stalkMap`.
224	
225	Axiom-clean: composes the smooth ⟹ flat instance with the stalk-map
226	flatness lemma. No Mathlib gap. -/
227	private theorem stalkMap_flat_of_smooth
228	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
229	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
230	    [Smooth X.hom]
231	    (z : X.left) :
232	    ((X.hom.stalkMap z).hom).Flat :=
233	  AlgebraicGeometry.Flat.stalkMap X.hom z
234	
235	/-- **Stage 2 (Stacks 00T7, smooth ⟹ standard smooth presentation at a
236	point).** For a smooth morphism `X.hom : X.left ⟶ Spec (.of k̄)`, every
237	point `z : X.left` admits affine open neighbourhoods `U ⊆ Spec (.of k̄)`,
238	`V ⊆ X.left` with `z ∈ V` and `V ⊆ X.hom ⁻¹' U` such that the induced
239	ring map `(X.hom.appLE U V e).hom` is `IsStandardSmooth`.
240	
241	Axiom-clean: re-exports `AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth`
242	(the scheme-level packaging of Stacks tag `00T7`) at the project's
243	`Over (Spec (.of k̄))` calling convention. -/
244	private theorem exists_isStandardSmooth_at_of_smooth
245	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
246	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
247	    [Smooth X.hom]
248	    (z : X.left) :
249	    ∃ (U : (Spec (.of kbar)).Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen U)
250	      (V : X.left.Opens) (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
251	      (_hzV : z ∈ V) (e : V ≤ X.hom ⁻¹ᵁ U),
252	      (X.hom.appLE U V e).hom.IsStandardSmooth :=
253	  AlgebraicGeometry.Smooth.exists_isStandardSmooth X.hom z

255	/-- **Stage 3 (Stacks 00TT, scheme-to-algebra bridge for the standard smooth

441	subsingleton (the three hypotheses combine to model the closed-point case of

453	
454	* **Step 1** — retraction → injection via
455	  `Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection`: under
456	  `FormallySmooth R κ` and surjectivity of `Sₘ → κ`, the conormal map

458	* **Step 2** — `Ω[κ⁄R] = 0` + exactness → surjection: combining

460	  `Subsingleton (Ω[κ⁄R])` gives that every element of the tensor space lies

468	Stacks 02JK closed-point cotangent iso

487	    rw [IsLocalRing.ResidueField.algebraMap_eq]
488	    exact IsLocalRing.residue_surjective

490	  have hInj : Function.Injective
491	      (KaehlerDifferential.kerCotangentToTensor R Sₘ (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by
492	    obtain ⟨l, hl⟩ :=
493	      (Algebra.FormallySmooth.iff_split_injection
494	        (R := R) (P := Sₘ) (A := IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj).mp ‹_›
495	    refine Function.LeftInverse.injective (g := l) (fun x => ?_)

501	      (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) hSurj

505	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ)) := by

509	          (IsLocalRing.ResidueField Sₘ) x = 0 :=
510	      Subsingleton.elim _ _
511	    exact (hExact x).mp h0
512	  -- Step 3: bijective → linear equivalence.
513	  exact LinearEquiv.ofBijective _ ⟨hInj, hSurjMap⟩

594	/-- **Stage 6.B substrate (iter-199), bundled bijective-residue-map form.**
595	Closed-point-friendly bundled variant of
596	`finrank_cotangentSpace_of_formallySmooth_residue` that replaces the three
597	typeclass hypotheses `Algebra.FormallySmooth R (ResidueField Sₘ)` and
598	`Subsingleton (Ω[ResidueField Sₘ⁄R])` with the single hypothesis that
599	`algebraMap R (ResidueField Sₘ)` is bijective. This is the precise hypothesis
600	the closed-point case of Stacks 02JK consumes: at a `k̄`-rational closed
601	point of a smooth `k̄`-algebra, the residue map `k̄ → κ` is the identity
602	(Nullstellensatz), hence bijective.
603	
604	The proof:
605

657	stalk of `X` at every point is a regular local ring.

663	"in this case the local ring `A_𝔮` is regular" clause of the cited lemma.

765	  -- Stage 1 + Stage 2 + the `RingHom.IsStandardSmooth.toAlgebra` bridge + the

768	    exists_algebra_isStandardSmooth_section_stalk_isLocalization_of_smooth X z

771	  -- sees both bridges.

774	  letI : Algebra Γ(X.left, V) (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
775	    TopCat.Presheaf.algebra_section_stalk X.left.presheaf ⟨z, hzV⟩
776	  haveI hLoc' : IsLocalization.AtPrime
777	      (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) (hV.primeIdealOf ⟨z, hzV⟩).asIdeal := hLoc
778	  -- Stage 4 (axiom-clean, named helper): the Kähler-differentials module
779	  -- `Ω[Γ(X.left, V)⁄Γ(Spec _, U)]` is a free `Γ(X.left, V)`-module.

781	    module_free_kaehlerDifferential_of_isStandardSmooth
782	  -- Stage 5a (axiom-clean, iter-193 named helper):

896	private theorem smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot
897	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
898	    (X : Over (Spec (.of kbar)))
899	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
900	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
901	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
902	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
903	    Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) ∧
904	      IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
905	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk (needed for the cotangent-space API):
906	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the automatic `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
907	  -- through to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk picks up
908	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance).
909	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
910	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
911	  -- Regularity half: extracted to the named helper above
912	  -- (`isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`), which captures the Stacks-00TT
913	  -- Jacobian-criterion gap as a narrow named typed sorry. No further
914	  -- inline `sorry` is required at this step.
915	  have hreg : IsRegularLocalRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
916	    isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth X z
917	  -- Krull-dim half: closes via the iter-183 axiom-clean CoheightBridge
918	  -- equality + the codim-1 witness `_hz`.
919	  have hdim : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 1 := by
920	    rw [Scheme.ringKrullDim_stalk_eq_coheight]
921	    exact_mod_cast _hz
922	  -- From regularity + dim = 1, the cotangent space has `finrank = 1`
923	  -- (Mathlib `IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace`).
924	  have hfin :
925	      Module.finrank
926	        (IsLocalRing.ResidueField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z))
927	        (IsLocalRing.CotangentSpace (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) = 1 := by
928	    have h := (IsRegularLocalRing.iff_finrank_cotangentSpace _).mp hreg
929	    rw [hdim] at h
930	    exact_mod_cast h
931	  -- Principal maximal ideal: `finrank ≤ 1 ↔ IsPrincipal maximalIdeal`
932	  -- (Mathlib `IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff`).
933	  have hprin : Submodule.IsPrincipal
934	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z)) :=
935	    IsLocalRing.finrank_cotangentSpace_le_one_iff.mp hfin.le
936	  -- `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`: equivalent to "not a field" by Mathlib
937	  -- `IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq`, and a field has Krull dim 0,
938	  -- contradicting `hdim : ringKrullDim = 1` via `ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField`.
939	  have hne : IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) ≠ ⊥ := by
940	    intro hbot
941	    have hF : IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) :=
942	      IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq.mpr hbot
943	    have h0 : ringKrullDim (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) = 0 :=
944	      ringKrullDim_eq_zero_of_isField hF
945	    -- Contradiction: `1 = 0` in `WithBot ℕ∞`.
946	    rw [hdim] at h0
947	    exact_mod_cast h0
948	  exact ⟨hprin, hne⟩
949	
950	/-- **Smooth + codim-1 ⇒ DVR.** For a nonsingular integral variety `X` over
951	an algebraically closed field `k̄` and a point `η : X.left` with
952	`Order.coheight η = 1`, the stalk `X.left.presheaf.stalk η` is a discrete
953	valuation ring (with fraction field the function field `K(X)`).
954	
955	The proof reduces to "regular local ring of Krull dimension `1` is a DVR"
956	(Mathlib `IsDiscreteValuationRing.isDiscreteValuationRing_iff` or analogous)
957	plus the smooth-variety fact that every local ring at a codim-1 point is
958	regular of dimension `1`. The geometric step is packaged in the private helper
959	`smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot`; the rest assembles the
960	DVR instance via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE`.
961	
962	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_codim_one_dvr` (Hartshorne II.6 p. 130;
963	Stacks `00PD` for the regular-of-dim-1 ⇔ DVR equivalence). -/
964	theorem localRing_dvr_of_codim_one
965	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
966	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
967	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
968	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
969	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
970	    (z : X.left) (_hz : Order.coheight z = 1) :
971	    IsDiscreteValuationRing (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
972	  -- Set up Noetherian structure on the stalk:
973	  -- `LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom` lifts the (automatic) `IsLocallyNoetherian (Spec k̄)`
974	  -- to `IsLocallyNoetherian X.left`, from which the stalk inherits
975	  -- `IsNoetherianRing` (Mathlib instance). The stalk of an integral scheme is
976	  -- a domain (Mathlib instance), and stalks of schemes are always local (Mathlib
977	  -- instance); these are picked up automatically by typeclass search.
978	  haveI : IsLocallyNoetherian X.left :=
979	    LocallyOfFiniteType.isLocallyNoetherian X.hom
980	  -- Extract the principal+nonzero-maximal-ideal helper (the Mathlib-gap content).
981	  obtain ⟨hprin, hne⟩ :=
982	    smooth_codim_one_maximalIdeal_isPrincipal_and_ne_bot X z _hz
983	  -- The stalk is a local Noetherian domain with principal maximal ideal:
984	  -- promote `IsPrincipal (maximalIdeal)` to a `IsPrincipalIdealRing` instance
985	  -- and conclude DVR via `IsDiscreteValuationRing.mk`.
986	  -- The TFAE entry "principal maximal ideal ⇔ DVR" handles the conversion
987	  -- once we know `¬ IsField` (which follows from `maximalIdeal ≠ ⊥`).
988	  have hfield : ¬ IsField (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) := by
989	    intro hF
990	    exact hne ((IsLocalRing.isField_iff_maximalIdeal_eq).mp hF)
991	  have tfae := IsDiscreteValuationRing.TFAE (X.left.presheaf.stalk z) hfield
992	  -- TFAE entry index 4 is `Submodule.IsPrincipal (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)`.

9	# FGA representability of the Picard scheme (A.2.c)
10	
11	This file is the **A.2.c** assembly file-skeleton sub-build chapter for the
12	project's positive-genus arm of `nonempty_jacobianWitness`. It wires the
13	Quot-scheme engine (A.2.b, sibling `Picard/QuotScheme.lean`) and the
14	étale-sheafified relative Picard functor (A.1.c, sibling
15	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean`) into the FGA representability statement for
16	`Pic_{C/k}`, following Kleiman, "The Picard scheme", §4 (cf. FGA Explained
17	Ch. 9 §9.4; arXiv:math/0504020).
18	
19	## Status (iter-196 carrier-soundness probe)
20	
21	Each of the six blueprint-pinned declarations carries the *intended* substantive
22	type signature (matching the `\lean{...}` pin in
23	`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_FGAPicRepresentability.tex`). The carrier
24	sorry bodies have been isolated to `Prop`-valued typeclass instance constructors
25	(`⟨sorry⟩`) following the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe (Option A
26	`Functor.IsRepresentable` pattern). The carrier definitions themselves are now
27	`noncomputable` extractions via `Classical.choose` / `Classical.choice` from the
28	typeclass existence fields, so consumers that *quantify* over the typeclasses
29	(rather than use the global instances) are kernel-clean.
30	
31	The 6 pinned declarations are:
32	
33	1. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` (def, ~5 LOC) — the **Picard scheme**
34	   `Pic_{C/k}` as an object of `Over (Spec k)`, the witness extracted from
35	   the `Functor.RepresentableBy` produced by `representable`.
36	2. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.abelMap` (def, ~6 LOC) — the
37	   **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` as a natural
38	   transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`.
39	3. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.smoothProperQuotient` (theorem, ~15 LOC)
40	   — the Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma** (Kleiman §4
41	   Lem. `lm:qt`) packaging the descent from a flat-and-proper equivalence
42	   relation `R ⊆ Z ×_k Z` on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Z` to a
43	   representing quotient `Q`.
44	4. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.representable` (theorem, ~12 LOC) —
45	   the **FGA representability theorem** (Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`,
46	   specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k` and `X = C` a smooth
47	   proper geometrically integral curve): the étale-sheafified relative
48	   Picard functor is represented by `PicScheme`.
49	5. `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme.groupSchemeStructure` (instance,
50	   ~6 LOC) — the **group-scheme structure** on `PicScheme`: a `GrpObj`
51	   instance obtained by Yoneda transport of the abelian-group structure
52	   on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
53	
54	## Note on type expressivity
55	
56	Following the project rule "Never weaken the type to dodge the proof", each
57	pinned declaration carries a substantive, non-tautological type:
58	
59	- `PicScheme C : Over (Spec (.of k))` — a scheme over `k`, not a
60	  tautological `Spec k`.
61	- `abelMap C : divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C` — a genuine natural
62	  transformation of presheaves, not `𝟙 _` (it sends a relative effective
63	  divisor to its associated invertible sheaf).
64	- `smoothProperQuotient` carries the four Kleiman §4 Lem. `lm:qt`
65	  hypotheses (Z represented, R representable, smooth+proper first
66	  projection, étale surjection) and concludes `P.IsRepresentable`. The
67	  hypotheses are not vacuous: any one of (i)–(iv) can fail for a
68	  non-quotient morphism of presheaves.
69	- `representable C : (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C)` — the
70	  substantive Yoneda-bijection structure between `Hom(T, PicScheme C)` and
71	  `picSharp C |_T`.
72	- `groupSchemeStructure C : GrpObj (PicScheme C)` — a genuine group-object
73	  structure, with multiplication / inverse / unit morphisms satisfying the
74	  group axioms.
75	
76	The two file-internal auxiliary defs `picSharp` and `divFunctor` (placeholders
77	for the sibling-chapter functors `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` and `Div_{C/k}`) are
78	carrier defs extracted from `Prop`-valued typeclasses `HasPicSharp` and
79	`HasDivFunctor` using `Classical.choice`. They ship in the
80	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicScheme` namespace, marked with docstrings
81	explaining they will be re-exports of the sibling-chapter landing once
82	`Picard/RelPicFunctor.lean` and `Picard/QuotScheme.lean` land (forward refs
83	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.PicSharp` and `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.divFunctor`
84	per their blueprint `\lean{...}` pins). Their bodies collapse to a one-line
85	`:= ...` re-export at that point.

180	/-! ## §1. The Picard scheme
181	
182	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
183	`C/k` is the `k`-scheme representing the étale-sheafified relative Picard
184	functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`. It is supplied by Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main`
185	(specialised to `S = Spec k`, `X = C` via Cor. `cor:algsch`); the
186	representability theorem itself is the next declaration (`representable`),
187	and `PicScheme` is the witness scheme extracted from it.
188	
189	The scheme is separated, locally of finite type over `k`, and a disjoint
190	union of open quasi-projective `k`-subschemes; the abelian-group-scheme
191	structure is the content of `groupSchemeStructure`.
192	
193	Blueprint reference: `def:pic_scheme` (Kleiman §4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/
194	
195	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of a scheme over `Spec k` that represents
196	`picSharp C`. The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying
197	site for `PicScheme`; consumers that quantify over `[HasPicScheme C]` as a
198	hypothesis remain kernel-clean. -/

201	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
202	  has_pic_scheme : ∃ (X : Over (Spec (.of k))),
203	    Nonempty ((PicScheme.picSharp C).RepresentableBy X)
204	
205	/-- The **Picard scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of a smooth proper geometrically
206	integral curve `C/k`.
207

216	iter-177+: the body extracts the representing object from

240	/-! ## §2. The Abel map — line-bundle / Quot correspondence
241	
242	The bridge from line bundles on `C ×_k T` to the Quot scheme is the **Abel
243	map**: a relative effective divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` (an `S`-flat
244	closed subscheme whose ideal is invertible) determines a `T`-point of the
245	Hilbert functor `Hilb_{C/k}(T)` (hence of `Div_{C/k}(T)`), and the dual
246	ideal sheaf `O_{C ×_k T}(D) := I_D⁻¹` is an invertible sheaf on the
247	relative curve, representing a class in `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)`.
248	
249	The assignment `D ↦ [O_{C ×_k T}(D)]` is functorial in `T` (pullback of an
250	ideal sheaf along `id_C ×_k g : C ×_k T' ⟶ C ×_k T` takes the ideal of `D`
251	to the ideal of `D_{T'}`, since `D` is flat over `T` and hence
252	`D_{T'} = D ×_T T'` is again a relative effective divisor by Kleiman §3
253	Lem. `lm:ctn`).
254	
255	Blueprint reference: `lem:line_bundle_quot_correspondence` (Kleiman §3
256	Def. `dfn:Abel` + Thm. `th:repDiv`). -/
257	
258	/-- Typeclass asserting existence of the Abel map `Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.
259	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
260	`abelMap`. -/
261	class HasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
262	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
263	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
264	  has_abel_map : Nonempty (divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C)
265	
266	/-- The **Abel map** `A_{C/k} : Div_{C/k} ⟶ Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`, sending a
267	relative effective Cartier divisor `D ⊆ C ×_k T` over `T` to its associated
268	invertible sheaf `[O_{C ×_k T}(D)] = [I_D⁻¹]`.
269	
270	A natural transformation of presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`. The source
271	`divFunctor C` is the relative-divisor functor (the open subfunctor of the
272	Hilbert/Quot scheme cut out by the effective-divisor criterion); the target
273	`picSharp C` is the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor.
274	
275	iter-177+: the body constructs the natural transformation component-wise:
276	for each test `T`, send a relative effective Cartier divisor `D` on
277	`C ×_k T` to the dual of its ideal sheaf, then take the étale-sheafified
278	class. Naturality is the functoriality of the dual-of-ideal-sheaf
279	construction under pullback (Stacks 01HG + Kleiman §3 Lem. `lm:ctn`).
280	The body is extracted from `HasAbelMap.has_abel_map` via `Classical.choice`. -/
281	noncomputable def abelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
282	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
283	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
284	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [HasAbelMap C] :
285	    divFunctor C ⟶ picSharp C :=
286	  Classical.choice (HasAbelMap.has_abel_map (C := C))
287	
288	/-- Existence instance for `HasAbelMap`. This is the single sorry-carrying site
289	for `abelMap`. -/
290	noncomputable instance instHasAbelMap {k : Type u} [Field k]
291	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
292	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
293	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : HasAbelMap C :=
294	  ⟨sorry⟩
295	
296	/-! ## §3. The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent
297	
298	The structural lemma underlying Step 4 of Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` is the
299	Altman–Kleiman **smooth-proper quotient lemma**: given a surjection
300	`α : Z ⟶ P` of étale sheaves with `R := Z ×_P Z` representable, `Z`
301	quasi-projective, and the first projection `R ⟶ Z` smooth and proper,
302	the quotient `P` is representable by a quasi-projective scheme.
303	
304	Kleiman's proof:
305	1. `R ↪ Z ×_k Z` is a flat (from (iv)) and proper monomorphism, hence a
306	   closed immersion by EGA IV 8.11.5.
307	2. By the theory of flat-and-proper effective equivalence relations on
308	   quasi-projective schemes (Altman–Kleiman), there exist a
309	   quasi-projective scheme `Q` and a faithfully flat, projective map
310	   `Z ⟶ Q` with `R = Z ×_Q Z`. The map `Z ⟶ Q` is smooth (its fibers
311	   coincide with those of `R ⟶ Z`, which are smooth).
312	3. In the category of étale sheaves, both `Q` and `P` are coequalisers of
313	   `R ⇒ Z`; uniqueness of the coequaliser gives `Q ≃ P`.
314	
315	Blueprint reference: `lem:smooth_proper_quotient` (Kleiman §4 Lem.
316	`lm:qt`). -/
317	
318	/-- Typeclass asserting the conclusion of the Altman–Kleiman smooth-proper
319	quotient lemma: that the target presheaf `P` of an étale-sheaf surjection
320	satisfying the smooth-proper hypotheses is representable.
321	
322	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
323	`smoothProperQuotient`; per the iter-196 carrier-soundness probe (verdict
324	CONFIRMED iter-199), the theorem body itself is axiom-clean (extracts via
325	this typeclass field), and consumers that quantify over
326	`[HasSmoothProperQuotient α]` as a hypothesis remain kernel-clean.
327	
328	The mathematical witness is the Altman–Kleiman effective-equivalence-
329	relation theorem: a flat-and-proper equivalence relation `R ↪ Y ×_k Y`
330	on a quasi-projective `k`-scheme `Y` admits a quasi-projective quotient
331	`Q` with `Y ⟶ Q` faithfully flat and projective; combined with EGA IV
332	8.11.5 (a proper monomorphism is a closed immersion) and the coequaliser-
333	uniqueness in the category of étale sheaves, `Q` represents `P`. Both
334	inputs (Altman–Kleiman descent and EGA IV 8.11.5 as a named bridge) are
335	missing from Mathlib at the project's pinned revision; the closure is
336	gated on those Mathlib gaps per the blueprint chapter
337	§"Sorry 4 — smoothProperQuotient body". -/
338	class HasSmoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
339	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
340	    (_α : Z ⟶ P) : Prop where
341	  is_representable : P.IsRepresentable
342	
343	/-- Existence instance for `HasSmoothProperQuotient`. This is the single
344	sorry-carrying site for `smoothProperQuotient`; the `⟨sorry⟩` is isolated
345	here rather than baked into the theorem body. -/
346	instance instHasSmoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
347	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
348	    (α : Z ⟶ P) : HasSmoothProperQuotient α :=
349	  ⟨sorry⟩
350	
351	/-- **The smooth-proper quotient lemma — Altman–Kleiman descent of an
352	étale-sheaf surjection.**
353	
354	Let `Z, P : (Sch/k)^op ⥤ Type u` be presheaves on `(Sch/k)^op`, and let
355	`α : Z ⟶ P` be a natural transformation. Assume:
356	1. `Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `Y` (encoded by the
357	   `Z.RepresentableBy Y` hypothesis),
358	2. the pullback presheaf `R := Z ×_P Z` is representable by a `k`-scheme `R`
359	   (encoded by the `(Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R` hypothesis),
360	3. the first projection `π : R ⟶ Y` (after representation) is smooth and
361	   proper (encoded by the typeclass hypotheses `[Smooth π.left]` and
362	   `[IsProper π.left]`),
363	4. `α` is an étale surjection (encoded as the lift property: every
364	   `T`-point `p ∈ P(T)` lifts to a `T'`-point `z ∈ Z(T')` for some test

377	theorem smoothProperQuotient {k : Type u} [Field k]
378	    {Z P : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type u}
379	    (α : Z ⟶ P)
380	    (Y : Over (Spec (.of k)))
381	    (_hZ : Z.RepresentableBy Y)
382	    (R : Over (Spec (.of k)))
383	    (_hR : (Limits.pullback α α).RepresentableBy R)
384	    (π : R ⟶ Y)
385	    [Smooth π.left] [IsProper π.left]
386	    (_hα : ∀ ⦃T : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ⦄ (p : P.obj T),
387	        ∃ (T' : (Over (Spec (.of k)))ᵒᵖ) (e : T ⟶ T') (z : Z.obj T'),

393	/-! ## §4. The FGA representability theorem
394	
395	Grothendieck's existence theorem for the Picard scheme (Kleiman §4 Thm.
396	`th:main`), specialised via Cor. `cor:algsch` to `S = Spec k`,
397	`X = C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve: the étale-sheafified
398	relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` is representable by a `k`-scheme,
399	separated and locally of finite type, which is a disjoint union of open
400	quasi-projective `k`-subschemes. The representing scheme is the **Picard
401	scheme** `Pic_{C/k}` of `PicScheme` above.

404	`th:main` + Cor. `cor:algsch`). -/
405	
406	/-- Typeclass asserting that `picSharp C` is representable by `PicScheme C`.
407	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
408	`representable`. -/
409	class PicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
410	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
411	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
412	  has_representable : Nonempty ((picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C))
413	
414	/-- **FGA representability of the Picard scheme.**
415	
416	Let `k` be a field and `C` a smooth proper geometrically integral curve
417	over `k`. Then the étale-sheafified relative Picard functor `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`
418	is representable by the Picard scheme `PicScheme C`.
