420	iter-177+: the body assembles the four-step Kleiman §4 Thm. `th:main` proof:
421	1. Stratify `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}` by Hilbert polynomial into a disjoint open
422	   cover `(P^φ)_φ`.
423	2. For each `φ`, bound twists by `m`-regularity to reduce to `P^{φ_0}_0`.
424	3. Factor through the Abel map `A_{C/k}` of `abelMap` to obtain
425	   `Z := P^{φ_0}_0 ×_{Pic^♯} Div_{C/k}`, a quasi-projective open of
426	   `Div_{C/k}`.

428	   represent each `P^{φ_0}_0`; reassemble over `φ` for the full
429	   `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}`.

431	The body is extracted from `PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable` via
432	`Classical.choice`. -/
433	noncomputable def representable {k : Type u} [Field k]
434	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
435	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
436	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSharpRepresentable C] :
437	    (picSharp C).RepresentableBy (PicScheme C) :=
438	  Classical.choice (PicSharpRepresentable.has_representable (C := C))

441	sorry-carrying site for `representable`. -/
442	noncomputable instance instPicSharpRepresentable {k : Type u} [Field k]
443	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
444	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
445	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSharpRepresentable C :=
446	  ⟨sorry⟩

448	/-! ## §5. The abelian group-scheme structure
449	
450	The Picard scheme `Pic_{C/k}` inherits an abelian group-scheme structure
451	from the tensor product of invertible sheaves on `C ×_k T`. The relative
452	Picard presheaf `T ↦ Pic(C ×_k T)/π_T^* Pic(T)` is already an abelian
453	group at every `T` (the quotient of an abelian group by a subgroup),
454	functorially in `T`; this lifts to the étale sheafification (sheafification
455	of an abelian-presheaf is an abelian-sheaf, since it commutes with finite
456	products in the value category), and then to a group-scheme structure on
457	the representing scheme by Yoneda.
458	
459	Blueprint reference: `thm:pic_is_group_scheme` (Kleiman §2 Def. `df:Pfs` +
460	§4 Def. `df:Psch`). -/

462	/-- Typeclass asserting that `PicScheme C` carries a `GrpObj` structure.
463	The single `⟨sorry⟩` instance below is the unique sorry-carrying site for
464	`groupSchemeStructure`. -/
465	class PicSchemeGroupObject {k : Type u} [Field k] (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
466	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
467	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : Prop where
468	  has_group_object : Nonempty (GrpObj (PicScheme C))
469	
470	/-- **The Picard scheme is a `k`-group scheme.**
471	
472	The Picard scheme `PicScheme C` carries a canonical `GrpObj` structure (a
473	group-object structure in `Over (Spec (.of k))`), obtained by Yoneda
474	transport of the abelian-group structure on `Pic^♯_{(C/k)ét}(T)` (sum:
475	`[L] + [M] = [L ⊗ M]`; inverse: `-[L] = [L⁻¹]`; unit: `[O_{C ×_k T}]`).
476	
477	The full abelian-group-scheme refinement is the conjunction of this `GrpObj`
478	instance with the `IsCommMonObj (PicScheme C)` predicate (commutativity of
479	the multiplication), which holds because tensor product of invertible
480	sheaves is symmetric; `IsCommMonObj` is a separate instance threaded on top
481	of `GrpObj` in iter-177+.

483	iter-177+: the body invokes the Yoneda lemma to transport the natural
484	abelian-group structure on `T ↦ picSharp C |_T` to the multiplication /

486	representable level functorially in `T`, hence on the representing scheme
487	by Yoneda. The body is extracted from `PicSchemeGroupObject.has_group_object`
488	via `Classical.choice`. -/
489	noncomputable instance groupSchemeStructure {k : Type u} [Field k]
490	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
491	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
492	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] [PicSchemeGroupObject C] :
493	    GrpObj (PicScheme C) :=

495

499	    (C : Over (Spec (.of k)))
500	    [SmoothOfRelativeDimension 1 C.hom] [IsProper C.hom]
501	    [GeometricallyIntegral C.hom] : PicSchemeGroupObject C :=
502	  ⟨sorry⟩

1295	/-! ## §7. Linear equivalence and the divisor class group -/
1296	
1297	/-- **Linear equivalence of Weil divisors.**
1298	
1299	Two Weil divisors `D, D' ∈ Div(X)` are linearly equivalent, written `D ~ D'`, if
1300	and only if there exists a nonzero rational function `f ∈ K(X)^×` with
1301	`D - D' = div(f) ∈ Div(X)`.
1302	
1303	`~` is an equivalence relation (reflexivity from `div(1) = 0`, symmetry from
1304	`div(f⁻¹) = -div(f)`, transitivity from `div(fg) = div(f) + div(g)`, all via
1305	`thm:principal_hom`). The quotient `Cl(X) := Div(X) / im(div)` is the
1306	**divisor class group** of `X`.
1307	
1308	On a smooth proper curve `C` over `k̄`, `thm:principal_deg_zero` shows the
1309	degree map descends to `deg : Cl(C) → ℤ`.
1310	
1311	Blueprint reference: `def:linear_equivalence` (Hartshorne II §6 p. 131). -/
1312	def LinearEquivalence [IsIntegral X] [IsLocallyNoetherian X]
1313	    [Scheme.IsRegularInCodimensionOne X] (D D' : X.WeilDivisor) : Prop :=
1314	  ∃ (f : X.functionField) (hf : f ≠ 0), D - D' = principal f hf
1315	
1316	end Scheme.WeilDivisor
1317	
1318	end AlgebraicGeometry

997	/-! ## §4. Milne Theorem 3.1 — codim-≥ 2 extension to a complete target
998	
999	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a complete variety has
1000	indeterminacy locus of codimension `≥ 2`. Specialising further: if `f` is
1001	already codim-1-indeterminacy-free (by hypothesis), then `Dom(f) = X` and
1002	`f` extends to a unique regular morphism `X ⟶ Y`.
1003	
1004	Blueprint pin: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne §I.3 Theorem 3.1 p. 16). -/
1005	
1006	/-- **Milne Theorem 3.1 (specialised) — codim-1-indeterminacy-free + smooth
1007	source + complete target ⇒ extension.**
1008	
1009	For `X` a nonsingular variety over an algebraically closed field `k̄`,
1010	`Y` a complete variety over `k̄`, and `f : X ⇢ Y` a codim-1-indeterminacy-free
1011	rational map, there exists a *unique* regular morphism `g : X ⟶ Y`
1012	whose induced rational map equals `f`.
1013	
1014	The proof combines two steps:
1015	* **Step 1** (codim-1 ruling-out): the indeterminacy locus has no codim-1
1016	  components, because at the generic point of any candidate prime divisor
1017	  `W ⊆ Z(f)` the DVR property of the stalk (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one`)
1018	  feeds the valuative criterion of properness on the complete target `Y`.
1019	* **Step 2** (codim-≥2 extension): at a remaining codim-≥2 point `x`, the
1020	  depth-≥2 property of the regular local ring `O_{X,x}` (Cohen–Macaulay
1021	  from `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` of `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`)
1022	  forces `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` and the pulled-back coordinates extend
1023	  uniquely. By the `CodimOneFree` hypothesis, Step 1 alone is already
1024	  enough on the dim-≤2 cases the project consumes; Step 2 is what extends
1025	  through the remaining higher-codim points to obtain `Dom(f) = X`.
1026	
1027	Uniqueness is the standard reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1028	(`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant`): two morphisms `X ⟶ Y` that agree
1029	on a dense open of the reduced scheme `X` agree everywhere.
1030	
1031	Blueprint reference: `thm:codim_one_extension` (Milne, *Abelian Varieties*,
1032	Theorem 3.1, §I.3, p. 16). -/
1033	theorem extend_of_codimOneFree_of_smooth
1034	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1035	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1036	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1037	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1038	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1039	    {Y : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1040	    [IsProper Y.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible Y.hom]
1041	    [IsSeparated Y.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType Y.hom]
1042	    [IsIntegral Y.left] [IsReduced Y.left]
1043	    (f : X.left.RationalMap Y.left) (_hf : CodimOneFree f) :
1044	    ∃! (g : X.left ⟶ Y.left), g.toRationalMap = f := by
1045	  -- Derived structural instance: the target Y.left is a separated scheme
1046	  -- (as `Y.hom : Y.left ⟶ Spec kbar` is separated and `Spec kbar` is affine
1047	  -- hence separated over the terminal).
1048	  haveI : Y.left.IsSeparated := by
1049	    rw [Scheme.isSeparated_iff]
1050	    have heq : terminal.from Y.left = Y.hom ≫ terminal.from _ := Subsingleton.elim _ _
1051	    rw [heq]; infer_instance
1052	  -- The substantive Milne 3.1 proof body reduces to showing Dom(f) = ⊤:
1053	  --
1054	  -- * Step 1 (codim-1 components of Z(f) ruled out) uses the valuative
1055	  --   criterion of properness on the complete target Y plus the DVR-stalk
1056	  --   structure at codim-1 points (`localRing_dvr_of_codim_one` above, gated
1057	  --   on `isRegularLocalRing_stalk_of_smooth`'s Stage 6 Mathlib gap).
1058	  -- * Step 2 (codim-≥2 extension) uses the depth-≥2 local-cohomology
1059	  --   vanishing `H¹_{x}(V, O_X) = 0` at a depth-≥2 point (Stacks 0AVF), a
1060	  --   genuine Mathlib gap at commit `b80f227`. The codim-1-free hypothesis
1061	  --   `_hf` alone suffices for Step 1's conclusion but Step 2 requires the
1062	  --   local-cohomology / `Module.depth` bridge from
1063	  --   `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean` (`cor:regular_cohen_macaulay`).
1064	  --

1067	  -- uniqueness follows from the reduced-and-separated agreement principle
1068	  -- (`AlgebraicGeometry.ext_of_isDominant` / `PartialMap.equiv_iff_of_isSeparated`).
1069	  --

1074	/-! ## §5. Milne Lemma 3.3 — pure-codim-1 indeterminacy into a group variety
1075	
1076	A rational map from a nonsingular variety to a group variety has its
1077	indeterminacy locus either empty or of pure codimension `1`. Combined with
1078	`thm:codim_one_extension`'s codim-≥2 conclusion, this forces the locus to
1079	be empty when the target is an abelian variety.
1080	
1081	Blueprint pin: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne §I.3 Lemma 3.3 p. 17). -/
1082	
1083	/-- **Milne Lemma 3.3 — indeterminacy into a group variety is empty or pure
1084	codim 1.**
1085	
1086	For `X` a nonsingular variety over `k̄`, `G` a group variety over `k̄`
1087	(a smooth group scheme of finite type, separated), and `f : X ⇢ G` a
1088	rational map, the indeterminacy locus `Z(f) := indeterminacyLocus f` is
1089	either empty (`f` is defined on all of `X`) or every point of `Z(f)` lies in
1090	the closure of a codim-1 generic point.
1091	
1092	The proof (Milne's): consider the difference map
1093	`Φ : X × X ⇢ G`, `(x, y) ↦ f(x) · f(y)⁻¹`. Then `Φ` is defined at `(x, x)`
1094	iff `f` is defined at `x`. Pulling back the maximal ideal of `O_{G,e}` gives
1095	a finite set of rational functions on `X × X` whose pole divisors are pure
1096	codim-1; their intersection with the diagonal exhibits the indeterminacy
1097	locus of `f` as pure codim-1.
1098	
1099	Blueprint reference: `lem:milne_codim1_indeterminacy` (Milne, *Abelian
1100	Varieties*, Lemma 3.3, §I.3, p. 17). -/
1101	theorem indeterminacy_pure_codim_one_into_grpScheme
1102	    {kbar : Type u} [Field kbar] [IsAlgClosed kbar]
1103	    {X : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1104	    [Smooth X.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible X.hom]
1105	    [IsSeparated X.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType X.hom]
1106	    [IsIntegral X.left] [IsReduced X.left]
1107	    {G : Over (Spec (.of kbar))}
1108	    [GrpObj G] [Smooth G.hom] [GeometricallyIrreducible G.hom]
1109	    [IsSeparated G.hom] [LocallyOfFiniteType G.hom]
1110	    [IsIntegral G.left] [IsReduced G.left]
1111	    (f : X.left.RationalMap G.left) :
1112	    indeterminacyLocus f = ∅ ∨
1113	      ∀ x ∈ indeterminacyLocus f,
1114	        ∃ (z : X.left), Order.coheight z = 1 ∧ x ∈ closure ({z} : Set X.left) := by
1115	  -- Milne's 4-substep proof body — see informal/milne-lemma-3.3.md for the
1116	  -- detailed chain (TBD). Each substep is project-side buildable on top of
1117	  -- Mathlib's Weil-divisor apparatus and the group-object API; substep 4
1118	  -- (pole-divisor intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1) is the
1119	  -- substantive Mathlib gap.
1120	  --
1121	  -- * Substep 1: construct the difference rational map
1122	  --   `Φ := (id × inv) ∘ (f × f) ∘ m : X × X ⇢ G`, dense on `U × U` for
1123	  --   any representative `(U, φ_U) ∈ f`. Needs the binary product on
1124	  --   `GrpObj`-objects and the right-monoidal-cat structure used in this
1125	  --   project (`MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, `MonObj.mul`).
1126	  -- * Substep 2: `(x, x) ∈ Dom(Φ) ↔ x ∈ Dom(f)` via the formula
1127	  --   `f(x) = Φ(x, u) · f(u)` for any `u ∈ Dom(f)` in a chosen open
1128	  --   neighbourhood — direct from the group-law continuity + openness of
1129	  --   the domain of definition.
1130	  -- * Substep 3: pullback of the local ring at the identity `e ∈ G`:
1131	  --   `Φ` defined at `(x, x)` ⟺ `Φ^* (𝒪_{G,e}) ⊆ 𝒪_{X×X, (x,x)}`. Uses the
1132	  --   `Scheme.RationalMap`-to-function-field machinery (the same gap that
1133	  --   blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction` at L744 below).
1134	  -- * Substep 4: on the nonsingular variety `X × X`, the pole divisor
1135	  --   `div(g)_∞` of any non-zero rational function `g ∈ Φ^*(𝒪_{G,e})` is
1136	  --   pure codim-1; its intersection with the diagonal is pure codim-1 in
1137	  --   the diagonal (Hartshorne AG 9.2, Krull's principal-ideal theorem).
1138	  --   This is the substantive Mathlib gap: Mathlib's
1139	  --   `RamificationTheory.PrincipalIdeal` provides Krull's HPP for ideals
1140	  --   but the scheme-level codim-1 intersection-with-diagonal lemma is
1141	  --   not packaged.

1144	  -- `Scheme.RationalMap` (also blocks `thm:weil_divisor_obstruction`),
1145	  -- (b) the codim-1 pole-divisor / diagonal intersection lemma (Hartshorne

1008	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, and `x ∈ 𝔪` an
1009	`M`-regular element, the depth of `M/xM` is exactly one less than the depth of `M`:

1072	      rw [depth_eq_smallest_ext_index]

1125	/-! ### Helper iter-198 Lane AB (axiom-clean): regular-element existence from positive depth.
1126	
1127	For a Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` and a nonzero finite `R`-module `M`, the assertion
1128	`1 ≤ depth_𝔪(M)` is equivalent to the existence of an `M`-regular element `x ∈ 𝔪`. This
1129	is a direct consequence of unfolding the depth definition to a regular-sequence supremum
1130	(Nakayama rules out the `𝔪 • ⊤ = ⊤` trivialisation in the `Nontrivial M` setting) and
1131	reading off a length-1 witness.
1132	
1133	This is the substrate companion to `depth_quotSMulTop_succ_eq_depth_of_isSMulRegular`: the
1134	two together implement the Stacks tag 00LE step "depth-drops-by-one for any regular
1135	element" plus its prerequisite "the regular element exists". -/
1136	lemma exists_isSMulRegular_of_one_le_depth
1137	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1138	    {M : Type u} [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M]
1139	    [Nontrivial M]
1140	    (h : (1 : ℕ∞) ≤ depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M) :
1141	    ∃ x : R, x ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R ∧ IsSMulRegular M x := by
1142	  -- Unfold depth: `𝔪 • ⊤ ≠ ⊤` by Nakayama in the `Nontrivial M` finite setting.
1143	  have hne_M : (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • (⊤ : Submodule R M) ≠ ⊤ :=
1144	    Ne.symm (Submodule.top_ne_ideal_smul_of_le_jacobson_annihilator
1145	      (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal_le_jacobson _))
1146	  have hdepth_unfold :
1147	      depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) M
1148	        = sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1149	          (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1150	          ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1151	    rw [depth, if_neg hne_M]
1152	  rw [hdepth_unfold] at h
1153	  have hlt : (0 : ℕ∞) < sSup { k : ℕ∞ | ∃ rs : List R, (rs.length : ℕ∞) = k ∧
1154	      (∀ r ∈ rs, r ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R)
1155	      ∧ RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular M rs } := by
1156	    calc (0 : ℕ∞) < 1 := by norm_num
1157	      _ ≤ _ := h
1158	  obtain ⟨k, ⟨rs, hrs_len, hrs_mem, hrs_reg⟩, hk_lt⟩ := lt_sSup_iff.mp hlt
1159	  -- rs has positive length (since (0 : ℕ∞) < ↑rs.length).
1160	  have hrs_pos : 0 < rs.length := by
1161	    by_contra h_neg
1162	    push Not at h_neg
1163	    have hk0 : rs.length = 0 := Nat.le_zero.mp h_neg
1164	    rw [hk0] at hrs_len
1165	    simp only [Nat.cast_zero] at hrs_len
1166	    rw [← hrs_len] at hk_lt
1167	    exact absurd hk_lt (lt_irrefl _)
1168	  rcases rs with _ | ⟨x, rs_tail⟩
1169	  · simp at hrs_pos
1170	  · refine ⟨x, hrs_mem x List.mem_cons_self, ?_⟩
1171	    exact ((RingTheory.Sequence.isRegular_cons_iff M x rs_tail).mp hrs_reg).1

1173	/-! ### Helper iter-199 Lane AB-gap1 (axiom-clean): minimal surjection substrate.
1174	
1175	For a finite `R`-module `M` over a local ring `R`, there exists a surjective
1176	`R`-linear map `f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M` of the **minimal possible rank**
1177	`n = dim_κ (κ ⊗_R M)` (where `κ = R/𝔪` is the residue field) whose **kernel
1178	is contained in `𝔪 • ⊤`**. This is the first step of constructing a *minimal
1179	finite free resolution*: iterating the construction on the kernel (which is
1180	itself finitely generated when `R` is Noetherian) produces successive
1181	syzygies whose differential maps each have image in `𝔪` times their target.
1182	
1183	This is the first substrate piece of gap (1) (Stacks
1184	`lemma-add-trivial-complex`) in the Auslander–Buchsbaum closure chain
1185	(`auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd`). It packages the basic
1186	**Nakayama-lift** of a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M` to an `R`-spanning family in `M`
1187	and reads off the kernel-containment from linear independence of the basis
1188	combined with the `1 ⊗_R -` evaluation.
1189	
1190	Mathlib substrate used (all axiom-clean):
1191	* `IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis` — Nakayama lift of a κ-basis.
1192	* `TensorProduct.mk_surjective` — the `1 ⊗_R -` map is surjective for the
1193	  residue-field tensor.
1194	* `Module.Basis.constr_range` — range of the linear extension equals span of
1195	  the chosen image set.
1196	* `Module.Basis.linearIndependent` — independence of a κ-basis.
1197	* `IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff` — `r ∈ 𝔪 ↔ residue r = 0`. -/
1198	lemma exists_minimalSurjection_finite_localRing
1199	    (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1200	    (M : Type u) [AddCommGroup M] [Module R M] [_root_.Module.Finite R M] :
1201	    ∃ (n : ℕ) (f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M),
1202	      Function.Surjective f ∧
1203	      n = _root_.Module.finrank (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R)
1204	        (TensorProduct R (IsLocalRing.ResidueField R) M) ∧
1205	      LinearMap.ker f ≤ (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) • ⊤ := by
1206	  set κ := IsLocalRing.ResidueField R with hκ
1207	  set n := _root_.Module.finrank κ (TensorProduct R κ M) with hn
1208	  -- Pick a κ-basis of `κ ⊗_R M`.
1209	  let b : _root_.Module.Basis (Fin n) κ (TensorProduct R κ M) :=
1210	    _root_.Module.finBasis κ (TensorProduct R κ M)
1211	  -- The canonical map `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` is surjective.
1212	  have hsurj_mk : Function.Surjective ((TensorProduct.mk R κ M) 1) := by
1213	    apply TensorProduct.mk_surjective
1214	    exact Ideal.Quotient.mk_surjective
1215	  -- Lift each basis element to a representative in M.
1216	  choose lift hlift using hsurj_mk
1217	  let m : Fin n → M := fun i => lift (b i)
1218	  have hm : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i = b i := fun i => hlift (b i)
1219	  -- Define `f` by sending each standard basis vector of `Fin n → R` to `m i`.
1220	  let f : (Fin n → R) →ₗ[R] M := (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R m
1221	  -- Evaluation: `f x = Σ x i • m i`.
1222	  have hf_eval : ∀ x : Fin n → R, f x = ∑ i, x i • m i := by
1223	    intro x
1224	    rw [show f x = ((Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).constr R) m x from rfl,
1225	        _root_.Module.Basis.constr_apply]
1226	    have h : (Pi.basisFun R (Fin n)).repr x = Finsupp.equivFunOnFinite.symm x := by
1227	      ext i; rw [Pi.basisFun_repr]; rfl
1228	    rw [h, Finsupp.sum_fintype _ _ (by intros; simp)]
1229	    exact Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => by simp)
1230	  -- Range = span of `m`.
1231	  have hf_range : LinearMap.range f = Submodule.span R (Set.range m) :=
1232	    _root_.Module.Basis.constr_range _ _
1233	  -- Nakayama: span of `m i` equals all of `M`.
1234	  have hspan : Submodule.span R (Set.range m) = ⊤ :=
1235	    IsLocalRing.span_eq_top_of_tmul_eq_basis m b hm
1236	  refine ⟨n, f, ?_, rfl, ?_⟩
1237	  · exact LinearMap.range_eq_top.mp (by rw [hf_range, hspan])
1238	  · -- Kernel containment in `𝔪 • ⊤`.
1239	    intro x hx
1240	    have hfx : f x = 0 := hx
1241	    rw [hf_eval] at hfx
1242	    -- Apply `(1 : κ) ⊗_R -` to `Σ x i • m i = 0`.
1243	    have h1 : (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (∑ i, x i • m i) = (0 : TensorProduct R κ M) := by
1244	      rw [hfx]; exact TensorProduct.tmul_zero _ _
1245	    rw [TensorProduct.tmul_sum] at h1
1246	    -- Rewrite each summand: `1 ⊗_R (x i • m i) = residue(x i) • b i`.
1247	    have hrewrite : ∀ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i)
1248	        = (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i := by
1249	      intro i
1250	      rw [show ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1251	          = x i • ((1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] m i) from
1252	        (TensorProduct.tmul_smul (R := R) (x i) (1 : κ) (m i))]
1253	      rw [hm i]; rfl
1254	    rw [show (∑ i, (1 : κ) ⊗ₜ[R] (x i • m i))
1255	        = ∑ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) • b i from
1256	      Finset.sum_congr rfl (fun i _ => hrewrite i)] at h1
1257	    -- Linear independence of `b` forces each `residue (x i) = 0`.
1258	    have hlin : LinearIndependent κ b := b.linearIndependent
1259	    have hall : ∀ i, (IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) : κ) = 0 := by
1260	      have := Fintype.linearIndependent_iff.mp hlin
1261	        (fun i => IsLocalRing.residue R (x i)) h1
1262	      exact fun i => this i
1263	    -- Convert each component-in-𝔪 to `x ∈ 𝔪 • ⊤` via `Pi.single` decomposition.
1264	    have hx_pi : ∀ i, x i ∈ IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R := by
1265	      intro i
1266	      have : IsLocalRing.residue R (x i) = 0 := hall i
1267	      rwa [IsLocalRing.residue_eq_zero_iff] at this
1268	    rw [show x = ∑ i, Pi.single i (x i) from (Finset.univ_sum_single x).symm]
1269	    refine Submodule.sum_mem _ ?_
1270	    intro i _
1271	    have hsingle :
1272	        (Pi.single i (x i) : Fin n → R)
1273	          = (x i) • (Pi.single i (1 : R) : Fin n → R) := by
1274	      ext j; by_cases hij : i = j <;> simp [Pi.single, Function.update, hij]
1275	    rw [hsingle]
1276	    exact Submodule.smul_mem_smul (hx_pi i) trivial
1277	
1278	end Module

1280	/-! ## §5. The Auslander–Buchsbaum formula
1281	
1282	For a nonzero finite module `M` of finite projective dimension over a
1283	Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)`, the **Auslander–Buchsbaum formula** reads
1284	```
1285	  pd_R(M) + depth(M) = depth(R)
1286	```
1287	(Stacks tag 090V). The proof inducts on `depth(M)`: the base case
1288	`depth(M) = 0` uses a minimal finite free resolution of `M` and the
1289	"what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF) plus iterated application of the
1290	depth-on-a-short-exact-sequence lemma (§4) to bound `depth(R)` against the
1291	resolution length; the inductive step picks a common non-zero-divisor
1292	`x ∈ 𝔪` on both `R` and `M`, applies the snake lemma to obtain a minimal
1293	finite free resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the same length, and uses
1294	the inductive hypothesis on `M/xM` over `R/xR`.
1295	
1296	Blueprint reference: `thm:auslander_buchsbaum` (Stacks tag 090V). -/
1297	
1298	/-! ### Lane G iter-195 structural carving: inductive-step helper.
1299	
1300	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula is
1301	extracted into the helper `auslander_buchsbaum_formula_succ_pd` below. This
1302	isolates the entire substrate-gap content of the inductive step into a
1303	single named typed-`sorry` declaration with a precise iter-196+
1304	re-engagement plan, rather than an opaque inline `sorry` at the case-split
1305	site. The main theorem then dispatches the `n > 0` branch by a one-line
1306	`exact` call to the helper.
1307	
1308	Per the iter-195 Lane G directive (OFF-CRITICAL-PATH minimal dispatch),
1309	this is a **structural commit** (option (b): carving with iter-196 re-
1310	engagement timeline). The HARD BAR is met; closure is deferred. -/
1311	
1312	/-- **Lane G iter-195 structural carving (typed-`sorry` substrate-gap helper).**
1313	
1314	The `pd_R(M) = k + 1` inductive step of the Auslander–Buchsbaum formula.
1315	
1316	**Blueprint proof shape** (`sec:ab_main` inductive step, Stacks 090V).
1317	Strong induction on `depth(M)`:
1318	
1319	* **Sub-case `depth(M) = 0`.** Let `e := pd_R(M) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1320	  `lemma-add-trivial-complex` (minimal-finite-free-resolution carving),
1321	  `M` admits a minimal resolution
1322	  `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → ⋯ → R^{n_0} → M → 0` with all matrix
1323	  coefficients in `𝔪`. The "what is exact" criterion (Stacks 00MF)
1324	  gives `depth(R) ≥ e`. Iterating `depth_of_short_exact` on the
1325	  short-exact splits `0 → R^{n_e} → R^{n_{e-1}} → K_{e-2} → 0`, …,
1326	  `0 → K_0 → R^{n_0} → M → 0` gives
1327	  `depth(K_{e-c}) ≥ depth(R) - (c - 1)` and finally
1328	  `depth(M) ≥ depth(R) - e`. Since `depth(M) = 0`, also `depth(R) ≤ e`,
1329	  hence `depth(R) = e = (k + 1) + depth(M)`.
1330	
1331	* **Sub-case `depth(M) > 0`.** Pick `x ∈ 𝔪` a common NZD on both `R`
1332	  and `M` (Stacks `lemma-depth-sum-direct-sum` ⟹ `depth(R ⊕ M) > 0`,
1333	  hence a common NZD via prime avoidance over `Ass(R) ∪ Ass(M)`). The
1334	  snake lemma applied to multiplication by `x` on a minimal resolution
1335	  of `M` produces a minimal resolution of `M/xM` over `R/xR` of the
1336	  same length, so `pd_{R/xR}(M/xM) = k + 1`. By Stacks
1337	  `lemma-depth-drops-by-one`, `depth(R/xR) = depth(R) - 1` and
1338	  `depth(M/xM) = depth(M) - 1`, with regular sequences in `𝔪`
1339	  corresponding to those in `𝔪/(x)`, so
1340	  `depth_R(M/xM) = depth_{R/xR}(M/xM)`. The IH on `M/xM` over `R/xR`
1341	  gives `(k + 1) + (depth(M) - 1) = depth(R) - 1`, i.e.
1342	  `(k + 1) + depth(M) = depth(R)`.
1343	
1344	**Mathlib substrate gaps** (iter-196+ targets; pinned-commit `b80f227`
1345	audit per chapter `Albanese_AuslanderBuchsbaum.tex` L554-560). Gap (4)
1346	closed iter-198; gap (1) has its first-step substrate landed iter-199;
1347	gaps (2)-(3) remain:
1348

1518	/-! ## §6. Cohen–Macaulay local rings
1519	
1520	A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth equals
1521	its Krull dimension (Stacks tag 00N4). Mathlib `b80f227` has neither the
1522	predicate nor the class — this file is the upstream gap-fill.
1523	
1524	Blueprint reference: `def:cohen_macaulay_local` (Stacks tag 00N4). -/
1525	
1526	/-- A Noetherian local ring `(R, 𝔪)` is **Cohen–Macaulay** if its depth
1527	equals its Krull dimension: `depth(R) = dim R`.
1528	
1529	Encoded as a `Prop`-valued type class so downstream consumers can write
1530	`[CohenMacaulay R]` and use Cohen–Macaulay as a hypothesis. Mathlib at the
1531	pinned commit (`b80f227`) does not expose any Cohen–Macaulay predicate;
1532	this is the upstream gap-fill.
1533	
1534	iter-176+: the predicate is `Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R =
1535	ringKrullDim R`. For the iter-175 file-skeleton the carrier definition is a
1536	typed `sorry` at the `Prop` level — substantively, the predicate is the
1537	named equality, but we package it as a `class` so use sites are uniform. -/
1538	class CohenMacaulay (R : Type u) [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R]
1539	    [IsNoetherianRing R] : Prop where
1540	  /-- The Cohen–Macaulay equation: `depth(R) = ringKrullDim R`. The numeric
1541	  comparison is in `WithBot ℕ∞` after coercion of the `ℕ∞`-valued depth. -/
1542	  depth_eq_krullDim :
1543	    (Module.depth (IsLocalRing.maximalIdeal R) R : WithBot ℕ∞) = ringKrullDim R
1544	
1545	/-! ## §7. Regular local rings are Cohen–Macaulay
1546	
1547	The consumer-facing input for A.4.a: every regular Noetherian local ring is
1548	Cohen–Macaulay (Stacks tag 00OD). The direct proof: pick a minimal
1549	generating set `x_1, …, x_d` of `𝔪` (where `d = dim R`), use that `R` is a
1550	domain (Stacks 00NQ) to start an `R`-regular sequence, and induct on
1551	dimension — each `R/(x_1, …, x_c)` is again regular of dimension `d - c`,
1552	so `x_1, …, x_d` is an `R`-regular sequence and `depth(R) ≥ d`. The reverse
1553	inequality `depth(R) ≤ dim R` is the standard depth bound (Stacks 00LK).
1554	
1555	Blueprint reference: `cor:regular_cohen_macaulay` (Stacks tag 00OD). -/
1556	
1557	namespace CohenMacaulay
1558	
1559	/-! ### Helper 1 (axiom-clean): length-bound on regular sequences
1560	
1561	For a Noetherian local ring `R`, every `R`-regular sequence has length at most
1562	`ringKrullDim R`. This is the **upper bound** half of Stacks 00OD: it is the
1563	specialisation of the equality
1564	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) + rs.length = ringKrullDim R`
1565	(`ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular`) to the observation that
1566	`ringKrullDim (R / ofList rs) ≥ 0` whenever the quotient is nontrivial, which it
1567	is precisely because `IsRegular` rules out `rs • ⊤ = ⊤`.
1568	
1569	iter-181 Lane G: closed kernel-clean. -/
1570	private lemma length_le_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular
1571	    {R : Type u} [CommRing R] [IsLocalRing R] [IsNoetherianRing R]
1572	    {rs : List R} (h : RingTheory.Sequence.IsRegular R rs) :
1573	    (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim R := by
1574	  have heq := ringKrullDim_add_length_eq_ringKrullDim_of_isRegular rs h
1575	  have hntq : Nontrivial (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) := by
1576	    rw [Ideal.Quotient.nontrivial_iff]
1577	    intro habs
1578	    apply h.top_ne_smul
1579	    change (⊤ : Submodule R R) = (Ideal.ofList rs) • ⊤
1580	    rw [habs]; simp
1581	  have hnn : (0 : WithBot ℕ∞) ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) :=
1582	    ringKrullDim_nonneg_of_nontrivial
1583	  calc (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞)
1584	      = 0 + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by simp
1585	    _ ≤ ringKrullDim (R ⧸ Ideal.ofList rs) + (rs.length : WithBot ℕ∞) := by gcongr
1586	    _ = ringKrullDim R := heq

1609	```
